note: Complete Rune Magic chapter from AiG. Contains cult structure rules (p.69-74) and 367 Rune spells (p.75-122). Spells are Glorantha-specific miracles granted by cults. Each spell's cult availability is defined in the individual cult one-pagers, not here.

rune_cults.heading: Rune Cults
rune_cults.text: A Rune Cult is an association of individuals who gather together, providing worship or propitiation to a particular deity. The cult maintains the ritual and responsibilities for its god, offering up sacrifices and caring for its shrine. Each cult possesses a priesthood that officiates at sacred rituals and acts as the intermediaries between the cult members and the god.

temples.heading: Temples
temples.text: A temple is a holy place, consecrated and protected by a god or gods. They typically have their own guardian entity and sacred objects. Temples are supported by the local community, being an important source of specialised magical resources. Not all temples require a building; many Orlanthi temples for example are natural places that provide access to

priests.heading: Priests
priests.text: Priests are people with the full-time occupation of leading magical activities. In Glorantha, the title of Priest is a profession, rather than a cult rank. They are responsible for maintaining cult holy places and regalia, performing sacrifices and key rituals, requesting divinations and organizing the support of the cult.

rune_cult_structure.heading: Rune Cult Structure
rune_cult_structure.text: Gloranthan cults possess a universal hierarchy which divides their worshippers into three broad groups, as described in the following sections: Lay Members, Initiates and Devotees. Despite this apparent ubiquity, cults have their own rank titles, membership requirements and duties which can vary wildly.

lay_members.heading: Lay Members
lay_members.text: Most cults allow people to worship without pledging themselves to the god or learning its inner secrets. Such worshipers are referred to as Lay Members. There are no restrictions to joining as a Lay Member provided the character is in good standing within the community and none of their Rune Affinities are in direct opposition to those of the god. Thus it is not unusual to be a Lay Member of several cults. For example, even the most devout Humakti might take the opportunity to attend an Mastakos charioteer festival if it offers itself. But the warrior will be forbidden to join the cult of Chalana Arroy as a Lay Member, because the goddess's Life Rune is the antithesis of Humakt's Death Rune.

initiates.heading: Initiates
initiates.text: A character who shares at least one Rune with a god, and meets any other cult requirements, can become an initiate of that god's cult. Initiation into a cult is a serious step, for initiates pledge themselves to the focus of a divine entity. They must swear to follow the commands of the god as expressed by the priests of the cult, participate in the cult Holy Day ceremonies, and are expected to commit a significant proportion of their time and attention to that god alone. Characters can start out as an initiate if they possess at least five of the cult skills at 50% or better; one of which must be a Rune Affinity of the deity.

limitations_on_cult_rune_magic.heading: Limitations on Cult Rune Magic
limitations_on_cult_rune_magic.text: As a cult initiate, a character may directly use a Rune Affinity shared with his god to cast Rune Spells, relying upon the power of that god to create the magic. However, the spells available to the cult are limited to those utilised by the god in its myths, and are subject to any restrictions that the god himself has.

acting_like_your_god.heading: Acting Like Your God
acting_like_your_god.text: An initiate's fidelity to his or her god has a great impact on personality. Game Masters are encouraged to permit characters to augment skills with their Devotion (see RuneQuest, page 76), in situations where they act in a manner in keeping with the god's personality. Similarly, the Game Master should freely assign penalties against skill checks when a character has not been acting like their deity.

devotees.heading: Devotees
devotees.text: A character who wishes to be a devotee must be willing to dedicate their life solely to the service of the chosen deity, abandoning all other magic that does not come from that god, its subservient cult heroes or gifted by associated cults. To become a devotee is not easy; the character must pass difficult tests given not just by the temple hierarchy, but also by the deity itself. They are tested on their piety, knowledge of the deity's myths and their personal virtues. Characters only qualify for the chance to undertake these stringent challenges if they possess at least three of the cult skills at 100% or better, one of which must be a Rune Affinity they share with the deity.

feats.heading: Feats
feats.text: A feat is a magical cult gift which emulates one of the worshipped god's mythic deeds, whose scope is beyond even that of the most potent Rune Spells. When a character becomes a devotee, they are granted one feat automatically as part of their inauguration, and can learn additional feats by performing significant Heroquests (see page XX for more information on Heroquests), and spending a suitable number of Experience Rolls to master that ability, providing the deity in question exhibited said power during the time of myths.

creating_new_feats.heading: Creating New Feats
creating_new_feats.text: New feats can be 'discovered' by Heroquesting and uncovering truths long forgotten since the Great Compromise. Such quests are incredibly dangerous, but the rewards of finding new feats are invaluable, not only expanding the myths of a cult but also making the successful devotee the focus of a new sub-cult. Examples include heroes such as Gore & Gash, or Kalikos.

leaving_a_rune_cult.heading: Leaving a Rune Cult
leaving_a_rune_cult.text: Leaving a cult as an initiate or devotee is not easy and requires permission from the priesthood. If not granted or, worse still, they are cast out for transgressing cult tenets, this results in a magical attack from the cult's spirit(s) of reprisal. Even if the apostate survives the attack, he remains the enemy of that god and often of its cult worshippers.

cult_holy_days_and_high_holy_days.heading: Cult Holy Days and High Holy Days
cult_holy_days_and_high_holy_days.text: Gloranthan cults draw together their worshippers on seasonal Holy Days and yearly High Holy Days, to make sacrifices of both material goods and Magic Points to their god. This worship has several benefits. First and foremost it allows worshippers to re-enact the myths of the god, potentially allowing HeroQuests to occur. Secondly, the deity grants some of the power gathered from its worshippers towards 'free' castings of any Rune Spell an initiate or devotee knows.

subservient_cults.heading: Subservient Cults
subservient_cults.text: Many cults include the worship of minor spirits and dead heroes linked to its god, who would not otherwise be venerated or even remembered. Subservient spirits are those subdued or befriended by the god before time began and now serve out of obligation. Examples of this are Spirits of Reprisal who punish those that dare to abandon the cult, or transgress its rules. Cult heroes on the other hand were once mortals who developed new powers or skills in devotion to their deity.

associated_cults.heading: Associated Cults
associated_cults.text: Associated cults are those which have formed an alliance or relationship with the main cult. Within a pantheon gods often lend their aid to each other's worshippers, in gratitude or recompense for deeds performed during the God Time. Like subservient cults, this help can take myriad form, from knowledge of a skill to an alliance with a species of animal. In most cases however, the associated cult provides a Rune Spell that the worshipper can only utilise if they share the same Rune Affinity.

rune_spells.heading: Rune Spells
rune_spells.text: The following section provides updated and sometimes heavily modified versions of Rune Spells originally found in RuneQuest 2nd and 3rd edition publications such as Cults of Prax, Cults of Terror and Gods of Glorantha. During the course of RuneQuest history, innumerable Rune Spells have been created to grant individuality to literally hundreds of divine cults.

spells_alphabetical: Summon Minion of the Snake Eater

spells.name: Absorption
spells.description: Use the default Absorption miracle (RuneQuest page 264).

spells.name: Accelerate Growth
spells.description: Accelerate Growth accelerates the growth of vegetation, ageing it by a number of years equal to the Intensity of the spell. Under its effects, all plants in the area increase in size and verdure dramatically, although they cannot grow beyond their normal maximum size. The growth occurs over the course of several minutes, but remains permanent once the spell ends. The spell is normally used to establish orchards or rapidly spread forests, at the cost of the sprawling, tangled proliferation within the area of effect. Repeated use of this miracle can potentially grow a thick forest over what had been open fields a few days previously.

spells.name: Affix Darkness
spells.description: Affix Darkness must be cast at night, and lasts until the following sunset. It preserves the darkness in a volume up to the full area of the spell, depending on the location and desires of the caster. For instance, in the open the spell usually occupies a hemisphere with a radius equal to the Intensity in metres; whereas if cast inside a building, the caster could limit its extent to within the walls and no further. The affixed darkness remains exactly as dark as when the spell was first cast. Bringing a torch or other light source inside the area illuminates it as normal.

spells.name: Alter Creature
spells.description: When cast upon a creature or person subject to the Survival Covenant of Waha, it reverses their status: herd beasts become people and people become herd beasts. They do not change physically. A beast which becomes a person may convert their INS to INT and may roll 3d6 for CHA. They become omnivorous, preserve their previous skills and start with a Native Tongue skill of INT+CHA. On the other hand, when a person is reduced to the status of a beast, their INT is transformed to INS, they lose their CHA characteristic and all skills other than Athletics, Brawn, Endurance, Evade, Perception, Stealth, Swim, Unarmed and Willpower. No language remains to them, but they can graze upon vegetation and survive. Normally this spell is used to temporarily awaken a favoured herd beast to aid in a task, or to chastise people who have transgressed cult tenets. If the spell endures long enough, from application of a Feat or via the Rune Spell of Extension for instance, an awakened beast may learn other skills.

spells.name: Alter Spirit of Disease
spells.description: Alter Spirit of Disease must be cast upon a disease spirit prior to its infestation of a victim. It permits the caster to permanently change the disease conditions according to the Intensity of the spell (see Disease Condition table page 81). Note that this spell can be used to strengthen or mediate a disease spirit's conditions, potentially weakening such spirits if faced as guardians, or ruining a spirit combat prior to a victim succumbing. If the spirit is hostile to the caster, it gains a chance to resist the magic.

spells.name: Analyze Magic
spells.description: This spell reveals one or more truths concerning the function of a single magical item, entity or substance. The number of questions the caster may ask is equal to the Intensity of the spell, but must be used before the spell expires.

spells.name: Animate War Tree
spells.description: See Create Warrior of Wood, page 88.

spells.name: Appease (Deity)
spells.description: Appease is a ritual utilised to perform sacrifices to dangerous gods or spirits to placate them, as per the Propitiate spell (RuneQuest page 273). There are a myriad of different versions of this spell, and the required sacrifices depend upon the particular cult. For example, the Cult of the Bloody Tusk accepts copious amounts of grain, freshly spilled blood or magical items as valid sacrifices, to ensure that their giant boars remain tame and under tusk rider control. If the caster rolls a critical success on his casting roll, whoever provided the sacrifice is granted a temporary bonus to their lands, beasts or personal safety, provided they are within the area of the spell. In the case of Bloody Tusk, a bonus of half the spell's Intensity can be applied to a single characteristic, up to the species maximum.

spells.name: Arrow Trance
spells.description: This spell merges the caster's consciousness with their bow, augmenting their ranged Combat Style to unprecedented levels by boosting the Level of Success of any bow attack by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, a Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical; rolled Critical Successes cannot be boosted higher. Whilst under its influence, the user enters a trance in which only the bow and targets exist; only moving to establish a clear shot or find another enemy. They cannot wield any other weapon, parry, evade, use their bow as a club or even cast other magic, including healing. If the battle ends before the spell expires, the caster will stand at rest and wait for new targets.

spells.name: Asrelia's Cavern
spells.description: Asrelia's Cavern creates an invisible barrier, forming a sphere centring about the point of casting. Wind cannot blow through the barrier, rain and snow cannot penetrate it, and it blocks flames. Even a flood washes up against the barrier harmlessly. Spells can be cast through the invisible boundary, and creatures can cross it freely. Only non-living natural forces are barred entrance. Elementals can still breach it.

spells.name: Attack Soul
spells.description: This spell enables the caster to attack a single chosen target as if they were engaged in spirit combat. If the caster overcomes the target's Willpower, the target loses a number of Magic Points according to the caster's Devotion skill (see Spirit Damage table, RuneQuest page 201). Just as in normal spirit combat, the target can resist and attack back, reducing the caster's Magic Points. Unless one of the participants discorporates or is capable of discorporating, neither can possess the other's body. The target may not resist this spell's effect on him, though Absorption, Spell Resistance or similar magic might block it. Other magics such as Spirit Block or Spirit Screen (Spirit Shield) work normally to protect their users. Unlike spirit combat, the caster can break off or resume this attack at any time during the spell's duration.

spells.name: Attain Perfection Of
spells.description: All of the Attain Perfection spells are now Mysticism Talents (see Mysticism chapter, page XX).

spells.name: Attract Attention
spells.description: Attract Attention forces everyone within the area of effect to resist the spell or direct their attention towards the caster. Affected enemies are forced to redirect attacks towards the caster, whilst neutral or friendly victims merely pay rapt attention to whatever the caster does. If out of reach of their new target, enemies will attempt to close the distance or use some other form of ranged attack or magic instead - however this does not prevent them from defending themselves if attacked by a third party. Members of the same cult as the caster are immune to the spell's effect.

spells.name: Aurora
spells.description: This spell can only be cast whilst the Dawn Star is visible in the sky. Within the area shimmers pale, ephemeral sheets of light which infuse those inside its boundaries with a sense of hope and confidence. Those standing under the aurora are granted protection against the forces of Darkness and Chaos, reducing the Intensity of magic associated with either Rune, by half the Intensity of the spell. The spell ends when the sun rises, no matter how long the Aurora spell has left to go.

spells.name: Axe Trance
spells.description: Akin to Arrow Trance, this spell turns the caster into a near unstoppable warrior, provided they are armed with an axe. Any attack roll has its Level of Success boosted by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, a Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical, whilst rolled Critical Successes cannot be increased any higher. Under its influence, the caster is unable to utilise any other weapon, save for their Unarmed skill (although a shield could used to passively block), nor cast spells until the magic ends. Note that Axe Trance does not boost the Level of Success when parrying.

spells.name: Axis Mundi
spells.description: Axis Mundi creates a holy sanctuary which enables free passage of ancestral spirits to the mundane plane, without the benefit of a Visibility spell or application of the shamanic Trance skill. In addition, the manifested ancestor can freely converse, cast magic and even engage in spirit combat whilst inside the sanctuary. It takes time for the desired ancestors to show up, however. Games Masters should roll 5d6 to determine how many minutes of singing, dancing or other ritual invocation is required before they manifest.

spells.name: Bat Wings
spells.description: This spell causes a pair of large bat wings to sprout from the back of the caster, enabling them to fly at a Movement Rate of 12. The wings have an inherent Armour Point value equal to half the Intensity of the spell, Hit Points equal to the caster's arms, and if damaged they use the Winged Biped Hit Location table (RuneQuest page 443). Bat wings are tinted a particular colour relevant to the providing cult.

spells.name: Bear Fruit
spells.description: Use the Ripen miracle (RuneQuest page 275).

spells.name: Beastmaster
spells.description: This spell may be cast upon one or more non-intelligent animals. If the targets fail to resist then they will do whatever the caster directs for the duration, though they will refuse to obey obviously suicidal commands, and neither can they be commanded to fight on his behalf (but might do so instinctively). On the other hand, the beasts will not attack the caster. Beastmaster affects a number of creatures equal to the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Become (Specific Other Shape)
spells.description: This spell comes in many forms. It turns the caster's shape into that specified by the spell. They retain all their old characteristics and skills, except for SIZ, which the caster chooses. The new SIZ cannot be larger than usual, but it can be smaller. In return the caster gains all the abilities (or lack of same) of the new shape. There are multiple versions of this spell, each permitting only one type of shape, anything from an animal, tree, rock, a dirty shirt or even a pile of dung!

spells.name: Bee's Head
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a gigantic bee. This allows them the ability to gain nourishment feeding from flowers. In addition, fluids (including beer, honey, water, etc.) can be drunk by the recipient and stored internally without harming the liquid, up to a total volume equal to the Intensity of the spell in litres. The liquid can be stored safely past the spell's duration, but must be regurgitated before the user can ingest any other substances.

spells.name: Beetle's Head
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a giant beetle, with spiked mandibles. He gains natural armour on the head equal to half the Intensity of the spell. He also gains a Bite attack as per a karg beetle (page 182), Size L, Reach M, doing 1d8 damage plus his Damage Modifier. This bite attack can be used in addition to normal weapon attacks and parries, provided the opponent is within reach.

spells.name: Beetle's Limbs
spells.description: See Sprout Arms (page 113), with the addition that the new limbs possess natural Armour Points equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Berserk/Berserker
spells.description: Use the default Berserk miracle (RuneQuest page 266). Note that worshipers of pacifistic cults such as Chalana Arroy have the ability to bring a warrior out of a berserk rage. The chances for success are equal to an Easy Harmony Rune affinity roll for a pacifist of Devotee rank, and Standard roll for a pacifist of Initiate rank.

spells.name: Bind Ghost
spells.description: Other than the above changes, use the default Bind Ghost miracle (RuneQuest page 266).

spells.name: Bind Ship
spells.description: When cast upon a boat or ship, this spell slows the Movement Rate of the vessel at the rate of 1 knot per minute until it is completely stopped. When the spell expires, the ship may begin moving normally, though it must accelerate from a stop. The maximum size of the vessels which can be affected depends on the Intensity of the spell as indicated by the Bind Ship table. If the ship has sails, they are strained by this deceleration. The chance of breaking or damaging the ship's rigging is equal to the Wind STR or less on 1D100 (see RuneQuest, page 130), rolled when the ship comes to a complete halt. Bind Ship is resisted by the Seamanship skill of whoever is captaining the vessel.

spells.name: Black Breath
spells.description: Under the effects of this spell, the caster breathes out a shadowy mist of writhing tendrils which will poison anyone who inhales them. Those that fail to hold their breath are subject to a poison with a Potency equal to 10 times the spell's Intensity. The caster is immune to the poison's effects.

spells.name: Blast Earth
spells.description: When cast upon an area of land, Blast Earth scours and disrupts the soil, so that no plants within the area blasted may flower or bear fruit until the spell ends.

spells.name: Bless Animals
spells.description: This fertility spell increases the calving potential for each beast within the initial area of effect. Each birth produces a healthy calf or, rarely, twins. Nine of ten calves born to a beast influenced by this spell are female. This spell is usually cast only on the cult's High Holy Day of the year, so that it aligns with the mating period of the cult's animals. Thus it affects the offspring produced in the following calving season.

spells.name: Bless Crops
spells.description: Use the default Bless Crops (RuneQuest page 266), with the above changes.

spells.name: Bless Forge
spells.description: Bless Forge allows a smith to imbue tools, weapons and armour with greater than usual properties. Only a single project can be worked on whilst the magic lasts, but it increases the Task Rounds (RuneQuest page 100) invested to a number equal to the Intensity of the spell. Thus an Intensity 7 Bless Forge spell would permit the smith seven Task Rounds to fashion the item; which in all probability, will allow it to receive several Enhancements (RuneQuest page 101).

spells.name: Bless Grave (Lay to Rest)
spells.description: Once this ritual has been completed, it ensures the soul of a dead person passes into the afterlife smoothly and does not return as a bad ghost. Neither can their soul be summoned for questioning, bound as a sapient undead or brought back to life, unless the magic is performed by a worshipper of the same cult that cast the Bless Grave spell.

spells.name: Bless Home
spells.description: This ritual is performed over a home and affects it and the family who dwells therein. Family members within the blessed home are warded against malicious spells and hostile spirits which cross its walls, as if protected by a combined Countermagic and Spirit Shield of equal Intensity. The enchantment ends if ever the family hearth is shattered.

spells.name: Bless Thunderstone
spells.description: Thunderstones are potent magical weapons formed from palm sized flints or similar stones. Once enchanted, the thunderstone remains quiescent until thrown, slung or struck against a target, at which point it shatters in a shower of sparks. For each two full points of Intensity, the stone does 1d6 Hit Points damage and destroys 1d6 Magic Points of a struck target. Armour protects normally against the Hit Point damage done by the stone, and likewise the Magic Point drain is reduced by the Magnitude of magic protective spells. The caster may only regain the Magic Points invested in the stone once it has shattered or the spell ends naturally.

spells.name: Bless Woad
spells.description: Enchants a pot of woad sufficient to coat the skin of a single user, granting them protection against wounds and magic. The woad grants its wearer Countermagic and Armour Points equal to its Intensity. Each day that the woad is worn, it declines in potency by 1 point. If the user ever puts on armour or clothing, the woad immediately loses all its magic power. Magic Points invested into a pot of blessed woad do not return until the woad is used or the enchantment expires.

spells.name: Bless Worshippers
spells.description: This spell must be stacked with another theistic spell known by the caster. For each point of Intensity in Bless Worshippers, the other spell takes effect on an additional target, who must be a worshipper of the same cult. The spell stacked with Bless Worshippers is thereafter lost until the priest returns to a cult shrine or temple to pray for its return.

spells.name: Blinding
spells.description: This spell blinds the target entity if they fail to resist. Unless they possess other senses to compensate, any skill requiring sight becomes Herculean difficulty.

spells.name: Blood Feast
spells.description: Blood Feast requires the caster to ritually prepare a meat stew in a cauldron, using the body organs of several creatures. After the cooking has been completed, all cult members participating in the feast consume the stew and may donate a Magic Point to the ritual. The result is a spectre, an artificial undead spirit, its appearance conjoined from the hellish mix of the species and body parts in the stew. Use the base characteristics of a Haunt, but limit its abilities according to the organs included in the stew. Once created, the POW and subsequent Intensity of the spectre is based on the total number of Magic Points invested into its creation. The spectre does not recover Magic Points naturally, but must have them donated by the caster, or drained from victims if guts were included in its creation. The spectre persists for as long as the caster continues to maintain the Blood Feast from their Devotional Pool. In addition, every cult member who donated a Magic Point into the Blood Feast has their total Magic Points reduced by 1 until the spell ends.

spells.name: Blood Red
spells.description: This spell is no longer supported.

spells.name: Breathe Air
spells.description: As per the Breathe Water spell, but instead allows aquatic water breathers to emerge above the surface and survive.

spells.name: Breathe Water
spells.description: Use the default Breathe Water spell (RuneQuest page 266).

spells.name: Bump in the Night
spells.description: Creates disconcerting sounds capable of unsettling listeners, but only if cast at night or underground. Anyone who fails to resist becomes subject to the effects of a Demoralise spell.

spells.name: Buoyancy
spells.description: Each point of Intensity lifts 5 SIZ of creature, or if an inanimate object, its totalled corresponding mass on the Creature Size Equivalency table (RuneQuest page 327), to just below the surface of the water. Heavier objects will not float, though they will sink more slowly than if the spell had not been cast. Lighter objects will be raised to be about halfway out of the water, bobbing about. Unwilling creatures targeted by the spell may attempt a Swim roll to resist.

spells.name: Butterflight
spells.description: Butterflight grows huge colourful wings from the caster's back, enabling them to fly at a Movement Rate of 8. The wings have no inherent Armour Points, but the fluttering imposes a penalty of Formidable on attempts to hit the user when aloft. Hit Points are equal to the caster's arms, and if damaged they use the Winged Biped Hit Location table (RuneQuest page 443).

spells.name: Call Barracuda
spells.description: This spell causes giant barracuda within range to cease whatever they are doing and race to the aid of the caster. Assuming any are present, the number of barracuda summoned equals the Intensity of the spell. Treat the monstrous fish as average sharks (RuneQuest page 384).

spells.name: Call Founder
spells.description: See Summon Founder (page 115).

spells.name: Call Monster
spells.description: This ceremonial spell summons a deep-sea creature or group of creatures. It is cast by a group of worshipers, led by a priest. If the casting is successful, each participant in the ritual may expend as many Magic Points from their Devotional Pool as desired towards the spell; or a single Magic Point if they are only lay members. The total of these accumulated Magic Points must exceed the combined Characteristic scores (STR, CON, SIZ, INT, POW and DEX), of the monster or monsters being summoned, otherwise the ritual fails. Once completed, the creatures take 1d100 hours to arrive and are not under the control of the summoners, unless a separate Command spell is cast to do so. Sapient monsters may need to be bargained with for their services. Magic Points dedicated to this spell only begin to return once the duration ends or the monsters are slain.

spells.name: Cancel Light
spells.description: Creates a hemispherical zone within which all magical light is dismissed if the Intensity of the Cancel Light is equal or greater than the light spell.

spells.name: Carapace
spells.description: This spell changes the recipient's skin to hard chitin, replacing the natural Armour Points of his torso to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Carry (Specific Disease)
spells.description: This spell makes the target a carrier of one specific disease, exposing anyone they physically contact to the disease. It also causes the target to become permanently immune to the effects of that specified disease.

spells.name: Catseye
spells.description: This spell affects the eyes of the recipient so that they can see by any amount of available light. If there is a complete absence of illumination, vision is impossible; however, if light from a single dim spark exists, they see normally, up to the maximum range of the magic. Eyes under this spell reflect light as do a cat's.

spells.name: Cause (Specific Disease)
spells.description: If the target fails to resist, they contract a dangerous disease of a type known to the caster. The Intensity of the spell limits which disease condition it inflicts. Use the Disease Condition Cost table to reflect the spell's effect. Multiple conditions may be purchased if enough Intensity is available. Onset time and frequency of attacks is based upon the victim's resistance roll: on a Critical, the disease takes a day before manifesting, on a Success an hour, on a Failure a minute and on a Fumble it starts instantly. The inflicted disease continues until cured or the target eventually passes away.

spells.name: Cause Plague
spells.description: At the expense of their own life, the caster collapses into a putrid puddle of highly contagious, disease riddled slime. Anyone within the immediate area of effect is splattered by transformation and must resist the magic, but their Endurance roll is at a Hard penalty. Those that fail are both infected with the chosen disease (see Cause Disease) and also transmit it to anyone they approach within arm's reach whether physical contact is made or not. New victims must resist the disease at a Potency equal to the deceased caster's Devotion skill, or also become infected and carry it onwards. Even if the plague is contained or kills off the entire local population, the original spot the caster died remains a source for the disease until purified in some manner.

spells.name: Chameleon
spells.description: Use the default Chameleon spell (RuneQuest page 266).

spells.name: Chaos Feature
spells.description: This ritual takes 12 hours and permanently gives the target a chaos feature. If unwilling, the recipient must try to resist the gift. If affected by the foul magic roll on the Chaotic Feature table (see Appendix C starting on page 209). For each chaotic feature an individual accepts, there is a 10% cumulative chance of turning into a broo. This transformation may either be resisted nor altered, and is permanent once it takes effect. The probability for the transformation is rolled immediately upon the assumption of each chaotic feature.

spells.name: Chaos Gift
spells.description: For the duration of the spell, the caster gains a random chaos feature from the Chaotic Features table. If gained via the Moon Rune, this spell is subject to cyclical effects. Once this spell has been used, the recipient always registers as chaos-tainted by appropriate detection magics, abilities or spirits. On a critical casting success, the user can choose which chaotic feature they wish to obtain.

spells.name: Chaos Spawn
spells.description: This spell summons a wailing, moaning void appearing in mid-air. That flung into or otherwise entering this void is irrevocably lost, but 1d3 Turns later, the void leaves a random chaos creature in its stead. Roll 1D10 to determine what it is. All creatures have a chance to hit equal to the Devotion skill of the caster. Neither the POW nor SIZ of the arrival may be greater than the respective POW or SIZ of the sacrifice, unless no sacrifice is made, when a random gorp appears. At the end of the spell, the wailing void reappears, envelopes the summoned creature, then vanishes for good after another 1d3 Turns.

spells.name: Charisma
spells.description: Use the Enthrall spell (RuneQuest page 269).

spells.name: Chastise
spells.description: This spell punishes enemies or those that transgress cult tenets, as per the default Corruption miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

spells.name: Chill
spells.description: Chill causes the target of the spell to suffer the effects of freezing cold in their very bones. If not resisted, at the end of each Round the spell inflicts 1d3 internal damage to every Hit Location of the victim, ignoring natural and worn armour. Creatures with the Cold Blooded ability who succumb to this spell immediately become torpid.

spells.name: Chomping
spells.description: Chomping causes the recipient's jaws and teeth enlarge and sharpen. For each two full points of Intensity, the damage dice and weapon size of the target's bite attack are raised by one step.

spells.name: City Harmony
spells.description: This spell affects everyone in the area of effect and keeps them from getting excited or violent if they fail to resist. It counteracts emotion-affecting spells (such as Demoralize and Fanaticism) with a Magnitude weaker than that of the City Harmony spell.

spells.name: Clairvoyance/Clairaudience
spells.description: Clairvoyance gives the caster the ability to know what is happening in an area which has been previously studied for at least 15 minutes. Depending on their runic association, the user has full audio or visual perception (or both), as if physically present in the area. Until the spell ends, the caster is in full trance, completely insensible to their surroundings.

spells.name: Claws
spells.description: This spell turns one of the caster's arms, or tail if possessing one, into a large scorpion-like pincer. The claw does 1d8 damage plus any damage modifier, and has a weapon size upon the Intensity of the spell: 1-2 Small, 3-4 Medium, 5-6 Large, 7-8 Huge, 9-10 Enormous. If the caster lacks claw as part of their known Weapon Styles, they can use the Unarmed skill instead.

spells.name: Clever Tongue
spells.description: Clever Tongue increases the eloquence of the recipient so that they are a delight to listen to. Any failed roll of a verbal skill, such as Commerce, Influence, Oratory and so on, is treated as a success instead. Fumbles, normal successes and critical successes remain unaffected.

spells.name: Cloud Call
spells.description: Use the default Cloud Call miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

spells.name: Cloud Clear
spells.description: Use the default Clear Skies miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

spells.name: Coerce
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to force a number of individuals equal to the spell's Intensity to obey his will. It automatically succeeds against targets wearing enchanted slave shackles, but other victims' Willpower must be overcome. It takes a full round of concentration to issue new commands; this is done through a form of Mindlink, and does not require the caster to speak aloud or the victim to share a common language. The caster cannot make victims take any action that will directly cause injury; doing so immediately ends the spell's effect. The spell does not function against cult members who hold superior or equal status to the caster.

spells.name: Coin Wheel
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a block of gold, causing a coin to separate from the rest of the mass. Called a wheel in common Gloranthan parlance, these coins are commonly worth twice their weight of raw metal, each being worth 20 Lunars in lands where their legality is recognised. One ENC of pure gold provides the raw material for about 60 wheels.

spells.name: Comfort Song
spells.description: All those within range of the caster feel the magic of this Song of Power, which keeps all hearers from feeling pain, effectively anesthetizing the body while still permitting consciousness. It is often used during childbirth and after battles. Characters that would normally be incapacitated from the pain of injuries up to Serious Wounds are not, though damage is unhealed.

spells.name: Command (Specific Species, Monster or Spirit)
spells.description: Command allows the caster to take over the actions and will of the specified creature, which fails to resist. If successful then whilst within range the creature will be in a special form of telepathic communication with the caster. The caster must form a mental image of the actions that he wishes the creature to perform. Commanding a creature requires a full round of concentration.

spells.name: Command Worshippers
spells.description: Casting this spell causes all initiates and priests of the cult to hastily finish immediate business, gather equipment and report to the caster as soon as possible. Command Worshipper must be cast at a shrine or temple to work, and during this time the caster must remain in the same spot or else the spell disperses. It is generally used only for emergencies or for joyous celebrations.

spells.name: Community
spells.description: This spell guarantees easy communication of ideas, facilitating the sharing of new or troubled thoughts. It affects every individual within range who belongs to one of the same communities as the caster. All INT related skill rolls, providing the user knows that skill, are automatically successful when utilised to advance the common interests of that community. A "community" is assumed to be a group no larger than several hundred members, such as a family, clan, village, town district, cult, school, guild or even mercenary company.

spells.name: Conceal/Concealment
spells.description: Conceal renders the recipient both invisible and inaudible for the duration of the spell, provided they perform no offensive or destructive action. If so, the concealment temporarily fades, revealing them to observers until they cease whatever action revealed them, at which point they fade back into invisibility at the end of the round. For the purposes of combat, the recipient must first disengage from their opponent before they can disappear again.

spells.name: Consume
spells.description: If the target fails to resist, Consume inflicts 1d3 damage to a random Hit Location, ignoring all armour. On each subsequent turn of the caster, the target takes another 1d3 damage to the same place, continuing until the location reaches a Major Wound. At this point it is utterly destroyed, turned into grey slime, possibly killing the target if it was a vital location. If the spell ends prematurely before turning to slime, any damage done can be healed normally.

spells.name: Consume Mind
spells.description: This spell drains the entirety of the victim's memory, transferring it to the caster's mind and leaving the victim a mental vegetable. It can be cast only during the cult's holy day and takes a week to prepare for the ritual. Such diabolical efforts come at severe risk however, since whether the spell succeeds or not, the caster automatically takes 1d6+4 damage to their head. In addition they must also pass an unopposed Endurance check, else roll on the Consume Mind Backlash table. If the spell succeeds, the caster may learn any or all of the victim's magic and knowledge-based skills. Divine spells so obtained are reusable, provided they were also reusable to the victim. In such cases the caster can power stolen Rune Spells with Magic Points from their own Devotional Pool. Knowledge and Magic-related skills known by the victim at a level higher than the priest's own ability may also be gained. For each such skill already known, the caster may attempt a single improvement roll as if learning by experience, whereas new skills hitherto unknown simply start off at base value. None of skills or improvements gained in this manner can exceed the value of the victim's former skill level.

spells.name: Conversion
spells.description: This spell is no longer supported, as permanent Chaos gifts now always taint those who are not Illuminates.

spells.name: Corruption
spells.description: If not resisted, this spell transforms the victim into a gruesome chaos horror. At the start of each Round, roll 2d6 for each of the target's characteristics. If the 2d6 result is odd, subtract it from the characteristic. If the result is even, add it to the characteristic. The 2d6 roll is always subtracted from CHA. If any characteristic except CHA is reduced to 0 or less, the being becomes incapable of survival and dies immediately. At the conclusion of the spell, whatever values the characteristics reached become permanent, as are the physical deformities. Note: This spell takes over from the miracle described in the RuneQuest core rules, which itself has been renamed to 'Chastise'.

spells.name: Counter Chaos
spells.description: Shields the recipient against the effects of any special ability a monster or being may get from its connections with chaos. Thus if a chaotic creature has a Chaos Feature increasing its natural Armour Points from 3 to 9, it is reduced back to 3 for attacks made by the protected character. A chaotic monster with Regeneration cannot regenerate from wounds made by the protected character, and so on. Note that this effect occurs no matter whether the Chaos Feature is advantageous or disadvantageous.

spells.name: Couvade
spells.description: A spell for midwifery, Couvade is cast upon a pregnant female and the father, or suitable proxy, of the unborn child, both of whom must consent to be included in this spell and be present at its casting. If cast within the first season of pregnancy, all the pains and sickness of pregnancy will affect him instead of her. The spell's duration lasts until the child is born. During delivery, the woman suffers only minimal pangs, but the husband is prostrate with pain; ensuring the safety of both mother and child. This spell is especially important for trolls, as they have much difficulty in childbearing, especially with the large great troll births and with trollkin. This spell does not, unfortunately, enable the mother to be safe from birthing trollkin, but they are born alive and healthy.

spells.name: Crack
spells.description: This spell must be cast at a non-living, non-magical object, which can anything from a piece of armour or a weapon, up to a roof beam or statue. Fetishes containing spirits and objects with temporal spells cast upon them do not count as 'magical' in this circumstance, only items made from magical substances or those that are permanently enchanted, such as spell matrices. The targeted object immediately breaks into pieces, provided its SIZ is no larger than the Intensity of the magic. A Repair spell can fix the broken object.

spells.name: Create Bonfire
spells.description: Create Bonfire must be cast on an existing flame or fire, increasing its diameter to the value of the spell's Intensity in metres. Although the flames can spread and hence illuminate, the actual temperature of the flames does not increase. If no substance is available for the expanded fire to burn, it rapidly dwindles back to its former status. This spell has no effect on magical flames such as Fireblade or similar spells. It will however increase a salamander's size by one cubic metre per two full points of Create Bonfire, augmenting its Hit Points and protection, but not its damage or other characteristics.

spells.name: Create Fissure
spells.description: Create Fissure opens up a massive crack in the ground, parting the soil to either undermine a structure such as a wall or creating a physical obstacle difficult to cross. The dimensions of the chasm depend upon the strength of the magic, having a length equal to Intensity in metres, a depth of half that, and a width equal to one third of the length. Thus an Intensity 6 Create Fissure spell would form a crevasse 6m long, 3m deep and 2m wide. If the crack is cast to undermine a wall or similar construction, the structure loses 1d10 Hit Points per point of Intensity, ignoring structural armour (RuneQuest page 12). If reduced to 0 Hit Points, it collapses where it has been undermined. If the chasm is formed directly underneath a target who fails to Evade, they fall into it, taking appropriate falling damage (RuneQuest page 118).

spells.name: Create Flippers
spells.description: This turns the recipient's arms and legs into armoured flippers incapable of performing any manipulation skill. Any Swim skill checks are made one difficulty grade easier. His swimming Movement Rate becomes 6 and his speed on land is reduced to 1. The recipient's skin on his limbs becomes leathery, granting a number of natural Armour Points equal to half the spell's Intensity.

spells.name: Create Foe-Curser
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a totem involving the skull of some person or creature, often set into an alcove, crevasse or atop a post. After it is created, the foe-curser will trigger its magic on trespassers entering the area who register as hostile to the caster, community or cult the spell was cast to protect. The type of curse depends on the skull being used, and lasts until dispelled or the next Sacred Time.

spells.name: Create Ghost
spells.description: The Create Ghost ritual must be performed over the body of a recently slain victim and takes several hours to complete. The ritual tears the soul of the person from the path that the soul naturally follows after death, and causes it to appear at the location of its old body and attack the caster in spirit combat. If the soul's Magic Points are brought to zero, it is turned into a haunt (RuneQuest page 406), permanently bound at that place. If on the other hand the soul wins, it either possesses the caster's body to wreak havoc, or possibly forces them to commit suicide, depending on the whim of soul before it departs once more for its afterlife.

spells.name: Create Gorp
spells.description: Another ritual spell which is performed over the earth itself, it transforms a portion of the ground into a live gorp with a SIZ equal to three times the spell's Intensity. It does not however, provide any control over the chaos monstrosity.

spells.name: Create Great Market
spells.description: This spell enables members of the same cult to combine and overlap their Create Market spells so that its collective Area and Duration are based upon the combined Intensity of the participants. A modestly sized Create Great Market cast with seven Create Market spells of Intensity 6, 6, 7, 7, 8 and 9 would grant it an Area of 49m radius and a Duration of five days.

spells.name: Create Head
spells.description: This spell must be cast on the cult's Holy Day or High Holy Day. In this ritual, a living victim is decapitated with a silver garrotte and their soul trapped within the severed, living head. The head is placed in a mental connection with the caster, and its magic points become available for casting spells. All Folk, Rune, Spirit and Sorcery magic the victim knew are usable by the caster, who must use victim's own skills to cast and manipulate them. Any Rune Spells known by a head can be used until the head's Devotional Pool is drained, at which point the caster will lose access to its Rune Magic. In addition, heads whose souls have not yet descended into gibbering madness may be questioned by the caster, who must succeed in an unopposed Influence roll to gain a truthful answer. The duration of Create Head continues until the caster drops the spell, the head suffers a Major Wound or it is drained to zero Magic Points. Heads cannot recover Hit Points naturally, but must be healed using magic. Neither can a head regain Magic Points, unless the caster ritually recharges it with their own points at a cult shrine or temple. Depending on the personality and strength of will of the victim, each month the soul remains trapped in its own head it must succeed in an unopposed Willpower roll, else go insane; at which point the caster loses access to the head's magic and its knowledge. When the spell ends for whatever reason, the head decays into a stinking mass of corruption, and trapped souls which still possess Magic Points become Mad Head Ghosts, tied to the putrid remnants. Treat as Haunts (RuneQuest page 406).

spells.name: Create Market
spells.description: Create Market creates an invisible protected zone identical to Warding in most respects. The priest must use staffs one or more metres in height, carved with cult iconography to define the boundaries of the zone; this area may be of any shape, provided it lies within the limits of the spell. Unlike Warding it does not trigger on just anyone crossing the boundary, but only on those who enter the area with hostile intent, whether violence or theft.

spells.name: Create Neutral Ground
spells.description: As per Create Market.

spells.name: Create Shadow
spells.description: See Summon Shadow (page 116).

spells.name: Create Skeleton
spells.description: Using the bones of a long dead or deliberately excarnated body, the caster animates the skeleton at the victim's old level of competence, with an upper limit of the caster's Devotion skill percentage. It works in a similar manner to the Raise Undead miracle (RuneQuest page 274), except that it only quickens a single skeleton. In return the skeleton endures indefinitely, until the caster eventually allows the Rune Spell to lapse. The raised skeleton is calculated as per the main rules (RuneQuest page 384), but gains the usual bonus to its STR and CON equal to the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Create Warrior of Wood
spells.description: Causes a tree, which has previously been ritually dedicated on the cult's High Holy Day, to awaken and serve the commands of the caster, so that it becomes capable of motion and combat. Use the Awaken miracle (RuneQuest page 264), treating its Armour Points as 6.

spells.name: Create Whirvlish
spells.description: This ritual creates a Whirvlish from the soul of a person slain by the enchanter. The ritual must be performed under the desert sky and takes an entire night to finish. The person whose soul is to be transformed into the accursed spirit is murdered at the completion of the ceremony. See Whirvlish page 145.

spells.name: Create Wildfire
spells.description: This spell must be cast on an existing fire, whether merely a candle flame or a bonfire, increasing its temperature. The Fire Intensity (RuneQuest page 120) is raised by 1 for each two full points of spell Intensity. Although heat is increased, the size of the original fire is not. However, fires augmented beyond Intensity 5 explode from the rapid thermal release, scattering flaming fragments of whatever was burning to a distance equal to the spell's Range; this effect can spread the fire further if they land on readily combustible material. At the conclusion of the spell the original fire burns itself out, having consumed all its fuel and leaving nothing behind but ashes.

spells.name: Create Zombie
spells.description: As per the Create Skeleton spell, except that it raises a zombie from a corpse instead.

spells.name: Cremate Dead
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to fully destroy the bodily remains of one or more cult members after death, guaranteeing that the soul will not return to haunt the family as a ghost or worse. It also burns all goods sent along with a corpse, allowing the deceased to carry some weapons and other supplies into the land of the dead. Cremate Dead can affect as many targets as the Intensity of the spell. It can also be used on the animated skeleton, zombie, mummified, or even vampire forms of former cult members, though the target may attempt to resist the magic in this case. Failure causes the undead target to spontaneously burst into flame and collapse as they are consumed.

spells.name: Crush
spells.description: Crush infuses the recipient's blunt weapon strikes with overwhelming force. For each five full points of Intensity, the size of the opponent's weapon is reduced one step when parrying. Thus an Intensity 6 Crush spell would decrease the size of a Hoplite shield or Greatsword from Huge to Large, whereas an Intensity 10 Crush would reduce the same parrying weapons to merely Medium, making this spell exceptionally dangerous.

spells.name: Cure Chaos Wound
spells.description: Cure Chaos Wound is used to remove an affliction received from a chaotic creature or spell, for example reversing permanent changes caused by spells such as Chaos Feature or Corruption. It does not heal any damage per se, but can be combined with other healing spells to cure chaos-induced festering wounds, diseases or in the case of Broo, impregnations.

spells.name: Cure Disease
spells.description: This spell cures any disease currently afflicting the recipient, removing any conditions which are not considered permanent, such as Hit Point reduction due to Maiming or Characteristic loss. Note however that Cure Disease has no effect on disease bearing spirits which might be possessing the recipient.

spells.name: Cure Iron Burn
spells.description: This spell has been renamed to Resist Iron Burn (page 107).

spells.name: Cure Poison
spells.description: This spell is the same as Cure Disease except that it affects victims of poisoning.

spells.name: Curse of Thed
spells.description: As per Reverse Chaos (page 107), save that the spell may only be used against creatures of Chaos or those tainted by Chaos in some way. Provided the spell is maintained, the victim suffers the enduring effects of the curse.

spells.name: Dark Fear
spells.description: This spell operates much like Bump in the Night (page 80), but Dark Fear can be used in daylight to create a small area of demoralising shadow around the caster.

spells.name: Dark Walk
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to be totally invisible and soundless in darkness and shadow. The blend in is total, but launching an attack reveals the caster until the end of the Round. For the purposes of close combat, the recipient must first disengage from their opponent before they can disappear again.

spells.name: Darklight
spells.description: Darklight creates an invisible illumination which can only be seen by cult members. It must be cast on any item which is capable of burning and giving off non-magical light, such as torches, lanterns, braziers, campfires and so on. Non-cultists can see the dim violet flames, but the fire illuminates nothing to them. The spell ends when the fuel is naturally expended.

spells.name: Darksee
spells.description: Allows the recipient to see an environment alien to their senses. Diurnal creatures can see in the dark as if it were full daylight, and vice versa for nocturnal creatures. Darksee also cancels the effects of sunlight on trollkin and cave trolls, as well as negating the effects of the Blinding spell as well.

spells.name: Decrease Temperature
spells.description: Decrease Temperature lowers the temperature by 1d30 Centigrade per point of Intensity, with the possible result of inducing exposure to those within the area (RuneQuest page 129). If the temperature drops below freezing point, any precipitation will turn to snow and standing water will slowly freeze over.

spells.name: Decrease Wind
spells.description: This spell decreases the average wind STR by 1d10 per point of Intensity for the duration of the spell (RuneQuest page 130). This spell has no effect upon temperature or wind direction.

spells.name: Defend Against Chaos
spells.description: Defend Against Chaos helps the caster to avoid being overwhelmed by chaotic creatures or enemies possessing an affinity to the Chaos Rune. Any Evade roll used to outmanoeuvre chaos foes in combat has its Level of Success boosted by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, a Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical, whilst rolled Critical Successes cannot be increased any higher. Under its influence, the caster is usually able to stay out of reach whilst whittling down the opposition one by one.

spells.name: Defend Against Law
spells.description: As per Defend Against Chaos, but works on foes without a Chaos Aura, Chaos Feature or a link to the Chaos Rune.

spells.name: Detect Truth
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to tell whether anyone within the radius of the spell is lying. If lies are spoken, the speaker emits a dark, smoky glow visible to members of the cult.

spells.name: Detection Blank
spells.description: Detection Blank is cast to screen any object, spell or person from detection by Sense, Detect, or Find magic of equal or lower Intensity. Unlike other anti-magic protections like Countermagic and similar spells, it does not let the person doing the detection know that his spell has been blocked.

spells.name: Devour (Race)
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to ritually consume a still-living person according to the specific race or species defined. Common versions include Devour Elf, Devour Human and Devour Troll. Over the course of the gruesome feast, the caster absorbs Magic Points from the suffering victim. The amount gained cannot exceed either the Intensity of the spell or the victim's CON, whichever is lower. These Magic Points replenish the caster's own, and any surplus can temporarily exceed the caster's maximum, but dissipate once the spell ends.

spells.name: Devour (Substance)
spells.description: The caster may eat any amount of the indicated substance without harm. Thus someone who casts Devour Chaos could eat a gorp, and would take no damage from its acidic nature nor gain any chaotic taint; whereas Devour Plant would protect against thorns and plant-based poisons; Devour Fire would allow the quaffing of flaming oil without burns; and so on. In addition to any nutritional benefits, the spell ensures that all of the ingested substance is fully digested and out of the caster's system by the time it expires. Thus it is an invaluable way of dealing with regenerating monsters or substances which are harmful to the caster such as iron.

spells.name: Devour Book
spells.description: See Ingest Scroll page 99.

spells.name: Devour Spirit
spells.description: Devour Spirit allows the caster to consume the Magic Points of spirits engaged in Spirit Combat. Any damage roll inflicted upon the spirit is absorbed by the caster instead. Thus a caster currently at 7 MP facing a Haunt at 11 MP, who inflicted a 3 points of damage on the spirit, would steal those Magic Points, restoring them to 10 MP whilst the spirit is decreased to 8 MP. A spirit reduced to zero Magic Points under the effects of this spell is utterly destroyed.

spells.name: Diamond Edge
spells.description: When cast on stone cutting tools such as pickaxes or mallets, this spell increases the damage inflicted by an amount equal to the Intensity of the spell, but only when used against creatures or objects made of earth and stone. Thus an Intensity 8 Diamond Edge cast on a pickaxe (1d6) would allow the weapon to inflict 1d6+8 damage against gnomes, gargoyles or a stone fortification.

spells.name: Discorporation
spells.description: The caster temporarily detaches the spirit of the touched recipient, allowing it to become a spirit and travel from their body up to the spell's range. Awareness of the recipient resides in their spirit form, so that their body remains comatose until the spell ends. Unwilling recipients may resist. A discorporated character is treated in all respects as an ordinary disembodied spirit, thus they are not only visible but also recognisable. The discorporated recipient may travel through physical objects without harm and can swiftly travel to known locations within range. In addition they are still able to cast magic, although their spirit is also vulnerable to magical attacks. Any fumble rolled during their discorporate sojourn immediately withdraws them back to their body. If their body is killed whilst still under the effects of this spell, the dead recipient may attempt one task before being drawn into whatever afterlife awaits them.

spells.name: Dismiss Elemental
spells.description: Use the default Dismiss Elemental miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

spells.name: Dismiss Magic
spells.description: Use the default Dismiss Magic miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

spells.name: Divination
spells.description: Divination allows the caster to communicate with their deity, but must be cast within a temple or shrine to that god in a ritual lasting one hour per two points of Intensity. At the conclusion of the ritual, the caster may ask one simple question per hour invested in the ceremony. In return he or she either will receive an answer of up to seven words or a brief vision or dream. However, the Game Master should privately roll against the caster's Devotion skill for each question. A failure implies that the caster unsuccessfully interpreted the answer or dream properly, resulting in a misleading or puzzling answer. Note: Deities are incapable of revealing what they do not know. The only areas over which they have any familiarity are their personal experiences during the God Time; the last known location and activities of their initiates and devotees, as reported during that worshipper's most recent prayers; and general information concerning events within the god's sphere of control of their Runes.

spells.name: Divination Block
spells.description: Divination Block must be cast on either a single person or object. During the period of the spell, the presence of the recipient or any activities it is involved with, are obscured from magical observation. This obscurement affects history itself, so that Divination or any other knowledge spells cast after the fact cannot recall any details of what occurred during the period of the Divination Block. However, the existence of a Divination Block having been used is not concealed.

spells.name: Divine Intervention
spells.description: Divine Intervention is no longer used in RuneQuest 6th Edition. Devotees instead receive the ability to channel their god directly via Feats.

spells.name: Draw Beast
spells.description: When this spell is cast upon one or more beasts possessing INS instead of INT, up to a number equal to the spell's Intensity. Those creatures which fail to resist move toward the caster at walking speed.

spells.name: Draw Human
spells.description: As per the Draw Beast spell, except that it attracts INT-based races descended from the Man Rune.

spells.name: Drown
spells.description: Drown is only effective on air breathing targets, filling the target's lungs with water if they fail to resist. At the end of that Round and every Round thereafter, the victim must make an Endurance roll as per the Asphyxiation rules (RuneQuest page 108). Breathe Water or similar spells renders the target immune to Drown, but does not heal any Fatigue damage already taken.

spells.name: Earthmight
spells.description: The recipient of Earthmight will gain 1d6 STR and 1d6 SIZ per two full points of Intensity, which may exceed its species maximums. It takes a full round to grow to full stature. The magic is only effective as long as the recipient remains in physical contact with unworked earth or stone. If this contact is ever lost, the spell ends.

spells.name: Earthpower
spells.description: Once cast this spell endures until it is activated. It is triggered when the caster's Magic Points drop to zero, providing they are in contact with the earth; whereupon they recover a number of Magic Points equal to a dice roll of a size commensurate to the Intensity of the spell, and recharge their Devotional Pool by a dice roll of half that size (see the Earthpower Effect table).

spells.name: Earthtouch
spells.description: Earthtouch permits the caster to learn the exact STR, CON, SIZ, DEX, INT, POW and CHA of any entity within range, providing that both the target being observed and the caster are touching the ground. It also informs the caster of the target's current Hit Points, Magic Points and Fatigue level. Only a single entity can be investigated at a time and it requires a Combat Action to change to a new target.

spells.name: Earthwarm
spells.description: Causes an area of soil or rock to increase in temperature, by a number of °C equal to twice the Intensity of the spell. Earthwarm is normally used to protect fields and orchards against frosts, but can also be used to protect travellers in frigid climes when no other heat is available or even melt snow and ice to unblock mountain passes.

spells.name: Ecstatic Communion
spells.description: Ecstatic Communion must be cast on the cult Holy Day or High Holy Day. It is normally performed as part of a specific ritual embracing an aspect of that deity within the cult temple or shrine. All participants within the area experience sensual bliss unless they try to resist. Otherwise everyone involved, including the caster, are overwhelmed by the sensations of whatever ritual activity they are performing. Those under the effects of Ecstatic Communion feel no pain or discomfort, nor may they react to physical harm against them, but must continue the ritual to its climax. Only the caster may attempt to break free of the spell, requiring them to succeed in an unopposed roll against their Willpower. The pleasures granted by Ecstatic Communion are so addictive that anyone who succumbs to its power, whether willingly or not, will thereafter cooperate fully with the cult to experience the bliss again.

spells.name: Enchant (Metal or Object)
spells.description: All enchantments are now cult specific Feats which require a Heroquest to the other world to perform.

spells.name: Enhance Gustbran
spells.description: See Create Wildfire (page 88).

spells.name: Enhance Mahome
spells.description: See Create Bonfire (page 86).

spells.name: Erotocomatose Lucidity
spells.description: The spell affects both the caster and one intended recipient, who does not usually try to resist. Both the recipient and caster experience the ecstatic Oneness felt during the moment of sexual climax. The experience lasts for the entire duration of the spell. The experience is so strong that after it expires, both participants' Fatigue is increased by one level per two points of Intensity.

spells.name: Exchange Spells
spells.description: This allows the user to trade knowledge of one Folk Magic spell. It cannot be used with Spirit Magic, Rune Spells or Sorcery. The user must set up a Mindlink connection between himself and his customer. The participants agree upon the spells they will attempt to trade, one spell each. If the Exchange Spells succeeds, each participant loses knowledge of the spell they chose and gains knowledge of the spell their partner chose. A fumble indicates that both participants lose knowledge of their own spell, and do not gain their partner's spell.

spells.name: Excommunication
spells.description: Use the default Excommunication miracle (RuneQuest page 270). The spell requires a preparatory ceremony of at least an hour at a cult temple or shrine before it may be cast. The target of the Excommunication need not be physically present, since the caster is requesting the god to perform the act of severance. Neither does the target gain a chance to resist, since the deity will not comply with the request unless the victim is truly guilty of the crimes they are accused of. In addition to losing their Devotional Pool, the victim will be pursued by the cult's Spirits of Retribution.

spells.name: Extension
spells.description: Other than the reduced cost, use the default Extension miracle (RuneQuest page 270).

spells.name: Face Chaos
spells.description: When cast upon the recipient, that person stands their ground and fights any chaotic foe they face, even if ordinarily they would have run. If desired the spell may be resisted, but might lead to future accusations of cowardliness. This spell does not render the recipient immune to the effects of such spells as Demoralize or Panic, though they still will not flee.

spells.name: Face Law
spells.description: As per Face Chaos, but works against enemies without a Chaos Aura, Chaos Feature or a link to the Chaos Rune.

spells.name: False Form
spells.description: This spell gives the recipient the semblance of a normal human to all senses, including non-human senses such as Darksense. This spell also hides any cult mark the recipient may possess. It is used by members of the cult to pass as normal humans. The spell is usually recast each seasonal cult Holy Day.

spells.name: Fang of Wachaza
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a spear, javelin, dart or trident. The weapon's point(s) take on the appearance of old ivory while under the influence of this spell. Anyone struck and damaged by the weapon must resist, or they instantly die. Otherwise the victim simply takes the damage rolled.

spells.name: Fangs
spells.description: Fangs causes the growth of huge red teeth from the mouth of the recipient. Attacking with these fangs utilises the Unarmed skill of the user, and does the base damage of a bite attack (see Natural Weapon Damage table RuneQuest page 324), plus any damage modifier. Furthermore, the fangs secrete an acid, the strength of which depends on the Intensity of the spell: 1-5 Weak acid; 6-10 Strong acid; 11+ Concentrated acid.

spells.name: Fear
spells.description: As per the default Fear miracle (RuneQuest page 270), save that the result of the opposed roll is nuanced. If the target is overcome, refer to the Fear table to judge the results of their Willpower roll:

spells.name: Featherscales
spells.description: Causes feathery clouds of loose, sticky scales to coat the caster's body, increasing their natural Armour Points by half the value of the spell's Intensity. Additionally, when the character is hit by any weapon, that weapon becomes gummed up and covered with scales, making it blunt and padded. Such weapons henceforth do only half damage till cleaned off. Featherscales does not work if the caster is wearing armour.

spells.name: Fertilize
spells.description: This spell must be cast as a daylong ritual. Whilst maintained by the caster it restores to the soil the necessary organic and mineral vitality to support plant life, whilst neutralising salts and other poisons. It is often used in regions which have been over-farmed, or are unnaturally desolate such as the Wastelands. Each point of Intensity fertilises an area of one hectare enough to feed a large family.

spells.name: Fight Disease
spells.description: When cast upon a sick individual, this spell helps to fight off the effects of a disease, mundane or magical, or Disease Spirit they are suffering from. Whilst the spell is in effect, all attempts to resist the disease or possession are doubled, as if the difficulty modifier of the roll was Very Easy.

spells.name: Find (Specific Thing)
spells.description: This spell informs the caster of the presence of the specific substance, object or creature sought — illuminating it with a slight glow that can be seen only by the caster. This spell can detect all incidences within range, unless they are shielded behind a metre of solid material. The caster remains aware of the sought after presence for the duration of the spell. Each cult Find spell is specific to a single Rune-related thing such as fresh water, horses, enchanted objects and the like.

spells.name: Firedwell
spells.description: Allows the recipient to live midst volcanic conditions, so that no magical fire or heat will affect them. Additionally the spell permits them to see clearly through smoke or fumes, and breathe any noxious mephitic vapour. On the other hand, the recipient will feel chilled at normal room temperature and be susceptible to cold, taking double damage from magic of the Darkness Rune.

spells.name: Fireshield
spells.description: Fireshield reduces the damage of all fire-based attacks by half the Intensity of the spell. Thus an initiate casting the spell at an Intensity 7 would reduce the damage taken from a bonfire by 4 points, and be immune to the flames of a Firebladed weapon (+1d3 damage) entirely.

spells.name: Firespear
spells.description: This is cast upon a short spear, causing its point to burst into flame. The normal damage done by the weapon is replaced by 1d6 damage per two points of Intensity. The wielder of the weapon still adds his damage bonus when he strikes, and once the spell concludes the weapon emerges unharmed by the magical fire.

spells.name: Fix Intelligence
spells.description: This spell only affects members of the Elder Races linked with the Man Rune. If the target fails to resist the spell, his INT is permanently transformed into INS. The victim is now ruled by instinct, rather than reason. He remembers his former life and knows his friends and enemies. Essentially, he becomes a very cunning animal in humanoid form. The victim becomes totally incapable of using any knowledge or language skills, or using any magic except magic items which automatically function. He still has full use of his weapons, and those Standard skills whose default value is not based on INT. If this spell takes effect on a shaman, the shaman's fetch takes over control of the body, but remains limited to those skills and spells it itself knows.

spells.name: Flaming Bow
spells.description: As per the Firespear spell, save that each arrow shot from the bow is consumed by flames as it travels towards a foe, inflicting the burning damage but preventing the impale special effect.

spells.name: Flay
spells.description: This spell must be cast on a whip, flail or knout. Each two points of Intensity increases the base damage dice of the weapon by one step. For instance, a barbed whip does 1d3 base damage. Under the effects of an Intensity 5 Flay spell however, its damage would be incremented by 3 steps, to 1d8 damage instead. Note that bonuses to the base dice remain unaffected. Thus if the whip in the previous example had the effective enhancement it would start with a damage of 1d3+1, and be boosted to 1d8+1.

spells.name: Flight
spells.description: The caster may transport one person, or a thing, up to SIZ 20 through the air for the duration of the spell. An unwilling target may attempt to Evade the grasp of the magic in an opposed roll, and if successful the spell fails. The maximum Movement Rate of the recipient is equal to the Intensity of the spell, though running or sprinting increases this as normal (RuneQuest page 105). However the ambient wind does affect the recipient, blowing them along at a Movement Rate equal to the STR of the breeze, or slowing them by that much if they are heading into the wind. The source of the spell limits how and where the recipient may fly. The Air Rune allows freedom to travel anywhere, but at the expense of never stopping, else they will waft gently to the ground. The Moon Rune only allows recipients to travel to and from the moon in question, but permits them to hang motionless in the sky if desired.

spells.name: Float
spells.description: See Buoyancy (page 80).

spells.name: Flowers
spells.description: This spell allows the user to create flowers at will until the magic ends. During concentration, the ground and existing plants around the caster erupt with pretty flowers and sprays of unfurling leaves. If the site is suitable for a small plant's continued growth, it takes root and sprouts. If the site is not suitable, such as the chinks in stone walls or a tiled floor, the flowers wither when the spell concludes.

spells.name: Forlorn Hope
spells.description: All cult members within range who fail to resist are compelled to fight fanatically for the cult. Any sense of fear or self preservation is lost, replaced with a desire to spend their lives dearly for their god. In order to maintain this devotion however, the caster must remain with the affected group of lay members and initiates, exposing themselves to the same danger. Cult members wearing enchanted slave collars or shackles (page XX) automatically fail to resist this spell.

spells.name: Form Ice
spells.description: Form Ice transforms all open liquids into solid ice within its area of effect, down to a thickness equal to twice the Intensity in centimetres. The ice so formed can bear creatures with a SIZ up to three times the Intensity. The mobility of the fluid limits whether it can be frozen, with initiates only able to freeze the surface of static water such as pools or lakes, whilst devotees can freeze flowing rivers or the surface of the restless sea. Unless the ambient temperature is below 0°C, the ice will melt naturally of its own accord.

spells.name: Free Ghost
spells.description: This spell can only be cast upon the Haunt or Wraith of a deceased mortal. If it fails to resist, the ghost is unbound from its haunting spot and returns to the Land of the Dead.

spells.name: Fumble
spells.description: This spell is usually targeted against an opponent in combat. If the target fails to resist, then their next skill roll, provided it takes place within the spell's duration, will automatically become a fumble. The results of the fumble depend entirely on the skill being used.

spells.name: Gift Power
spells.description: Gift Power temporarily links the caster with a spirit, allowing him to transfer part of his Devotional Pool to augment the spirit's POW. The increase not only adds to the spirit's available Magic Points, but may also boost its Spirit Intensity. The maximum number of points the caster can transfer from his pool to the spirit is equal to the Intensity of the spell. Obviously the caster cannot access the gifted points of his pool until the spell ends, and even then he must recharge them first.

spells.name: Gift Spell
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to transfer the knowledge of any one Folk Magic spell to another person, or a spirit with the Spellcasting ability.
