The caster loses knowledge of the spell whilst it is transferred, but reclaims it once the duration of the spell ends, or he ceases to maintain it.

spells.name: Glow Spot
spells.description: This spell causes a beam of crimson light to descend from the Red Moon, illuminating the area around the recipient. This acts as a Glowspot, allowing all Lunar magic cast within its radius to function as it does on the day of the full moon. For this effect to work on ranged spells, both the caster and target must be within the radius of this or another Glowspot.

spells.name: Gnome to Gargoyle
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a gnome. It turns the earth elemental into a humanoid monster. The Characteristics and Attributes of the gargoyle remain those of the original gnome; however, it loses its ability to magically manipulate earth and rock. In exchange, the gargoyle gains Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell, plus the Hit Locations of its namesake (page 168). The damage each location can take is calculated by applying the gnome's total Hit Points on the Hit Points per Location table (RuneQuest page 14), noting that the gargoyle's arms have the same HP as its legs, whereas its wings have 1 HP less. When the spell expires, the monster turns back into earth.

spells.name: Great Parry
spells.description: Great Parry must be cast upon a shield. It grants the shield an infinitely large Size in terms of blocking damage from attacks, although Knockback still affects the holder of the shield. So the wielder of the shield, providing they succeed in parrying, could use a buckler to stop all damage from the nose horn of a charging rhino, but would still be knocked flying.

spells.name: Group Defense
spells.description: Creates a shadowy sphere centred on the caster, with a radius up to half the Intensity of the spell. All those contained within are protected within its veil-like barrier. Any creature attempting to pass this barrier is automatically struck in a random Hit Location, ignoring armour, with damage equal to the Intensity of the spell. If two or more casters link their Group Defense spells, the amount of damage inflicted by the conjoined barrier becomes the sum of the Intensities. Whilst the spell is active, the caster cannot perform any aggressive act.

spells.name: Group Laughter
spells.description: This spell ensures that everyone within the area sees the humour of every joke told, and old jokes that everyone knows seem funny again. Indeed those under its effects are even capable of enjoying practical jokes played on themselves. Due to its harmless nature, the spell's effects cannot be resisted.

spells.name: Guided Teleportation
spells.description: Guided Teleportation returns the caster to a specific spot sacred to the god who provides the magic, usually a shrine or temple. This spell has no range limitation, but its destinations are obviously limited.

spells.name: Hallucinate
spells.description: Use Illusion (RuneQuest page 271), save that the spell only affects the caster and those in Mindlink with him. The hallucinations are completely undetectable to anyone else.

spells.name: Hate Sky
spells.description: This spell causes all non-magical flames within the area of an Intensity equal or less than half the Intensity of the spell to be extinguished. Thus an initiate who cast this spell at an Intensity of 5 would instantly smother all flames up to and including campfires (Intensity 3 fires). Hate Sky also affects magical fires with lesser Magnitude, so a sufficiently potent casting would extinguish a Firespear or Elemental Summoning used to summon a salamander.

spells.name: Heal Area
spells.description: This spell heals any wound in a single hit location, including attaching a severed limb, provided it is cast within a number of minutes not exceeding the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Heal Body
spells.description: Use Heal Body (RuneQuest page 271).

spells.name: Heal Constitution
spells.description: See Cure Poison (page 89).

spells.name: Heal Wound
spells.description: See Heal Area (above).

spells.name: Healing Trance
spells.description: The recipient of this spell will fall into a deep trance, during which his bodily processes will be sped up by a multiple equal to the Intensity of the spell. Casting the spell with an Intensity of 8 on an injured warrior would speed up his natural Healing Rate by a factor of 8:1, resulting in the restoration of Hit Points in one eighth the time: so Minor Wounds would recover every 3 hours, Serious Wounds every 21 hours and Major Wounds every four days. Both the caster and the recipient remain in an unwakeable trance state until all healing is done. They must be fed and cared for as if they were catatonics during this period.

spells.name: Heat Metal
spells.description: As per Create Wildfire (page 88), save that it can only be used on a forge fire and does not explode.

spells.name: Heatblast
spells.description: Heatblast can only be used on earth which is at a minimum of 40 degrees C, either naturally via geothermal activity, or through the Divine spell Earthwarm. It concentrates this ambient heat, then releases it in a burst, affecting everyone in the area who fails to resist. The blast inflicts 1d6 damage per 2 points of Intensity to every Hit Location, although natural and worn armour will help reduce the burns. Casting this spell drains the surrounding ground of warmth, preventing further castings of Heatblast until several days have passed to recuperate its thermal capacity.

spells.name: Hide Fire
spells.description: This spell is cast upon a container of any type or size, for instance a man's hand, a burlap sack or a pail. It permits the container to hold an appropriately sized fire without being burnt or putting the fire out. Thus the caster could pick a candle flame off its wick and hold in his palm for the duration of the fire; or he could also close his fist and hide the flame — then when he opened it, the fire would still be there.

spells.name: Hide Wealth
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon an inanimate bundle of goods, with an ENC no greater than its Intensity. It causes the target to become invisible to everyone except the caster or to anyone watching as the spell is cast. Objects and magics hidden with this spell cannot be sensed, even with detection spells, but the presence of the spell of Hide Wealth itself could be. If the hidden goods are moved, the spell dissipates.

spells.name: Hie Wagon
spells.description: Hie Wagon must be cast on a moving wheeled vehicle, such as a cart, wagon or chariot in which the caster is travelling. It increases the entire vehicle's Movement Rate, including that of its draft animals, by the Intensity of the spell. Animals drawing the wagon return to normal speed if cut loose.

spells.name: Hold Course
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a ship upon which the caster is travelling. For the duration of the spell the ship continues on the exact same course in which it was set when the spell was cast. No directional changes can be made to the ship's course unless Hold Course is allowed to lapse or is dispelled. Likewise natural ocean going events cannot alter its course, although magic of a higher Magnitude can temporarily overwhelm it.

spells.name: Hurling
spells.description: All objects thrown by the caster have their range multiplied by the Intensity of the spell. The multiple affects all aspects of range, so a thrown javelin under the influence of an Intensity 5 Hurling would have a Close Range of 50m, Effective Range of 100m and Long Range of 250m. The spell does not increase range for purely projectile weapons such as bows and crossbows, but has a partial effect on blowguns and slings, halving its effect.

spells.name: Identify Scent
spells.description: This spell enables the caster to perfectly identify the nature of any familiar scent he encounters while under the spell's influence. It does not give range or direction, just nature. If the scent is of a substance or entity that the caster has never before encountered, he only knows that an unknown scent has been encountered.

spells.name: Impede Chaos
spells.description: Impede Chaos protects against the attacks and spells of chaotic beings. The spell imposes one additional difficulty grade against any attempt a chaos creature makes to harm the recipient. It has no effect on non-chaotic opponents.

spells.name: Incarnate Ancestor
spells.description: This spell summons the spirit of an ancestor to occupy the body of one of its descendants. The family member must be willing, and may be the caster. The summoned spirit will know all spells, knowledge and possible skills which it knew while still living. The spirit of the host body is temporarily suspended in the Spirit Plane and will return when the ancestor decides to leave or is exorcised. Friendly spirits leave when the job at hand is complete; neutral spirits must be bribed to do so using something like Gift Power or Gift Spell, whilst malign ancestors do not leave voluntarily, tying up points of the caster's Devotional Pool until it can be gotten rid of. This spell can summon a specific ancestor back a number of generations up to half the Intensity of the spell. Or it can be cast without naming the specific ancestor desired, in which case a random ancestor comes — a dangerous procedure. Note that powerful ancestors often have a POW Characteristic in excess of normal species maximums.

spells.name: Increase Temperature
spells.description: Increase Temperature raises the temperature by 1d30 Centigrade per point of Intensity, with the possible result of inducing heat exposure to those within the area (RuneQuest page 129). If the temperature rises above freezing point, any ice or snow in the area of effect will begin to melt.

spells.name: Increase Wind
spells.description: This spell increases the average wind STR by 1d10 per point of Intensity for the duration of the spell (RuneQuest page 130). This spell has no effect upon temperature or wind direction.

spells.name: Induce Uzdo
spells.description: This spell is used when a troll woman wishes to give birth to a Great Troll. It summons one of Karrg's followers, an Intensity 4 darkness spirit with a POW of 1d6+24, INT of 2d6+6, CHA of 2d6+6 and Darkness Rune affinity of 100%. The female troll then steps forward and the spirit envelopes her in its form, initiating Spirit Combat. If she manages to inflict damage on the spirit it ceases its attack out of respect for her strength and joins with her. Several hours later, she emerges from the darkness and the spirit returns to the underworld. The troll woman is now pregnant, and gives birth a year later to an uzdo infant. Those that fail to overcome Karrg's follower even once in Spirit Combat before running out of Magic Points have their soul Sundered from their body and die (RuneQuest page 212).

spells.name: Ingest Scroll
spells.description: This spell is cast on text that the caster holds in his hands or is touching. This could be a scroll, the open pages of a book, an inscription carved in wood or stone and so on. It causes the information to be translated and transferred to the caster's mind, destroying any trace of it on the page or scroll, or crumbling the surface if carved into more permanent material. The wash of knowledge is so overwhelming that the caster must succeed in a roll against his Willpower or go into shock for 1d6 rounds; after which the caster must make and pass a second roll to prevent losing all the information gained.

spells.name: Insect Song
spells.description: The recipient can, at will, create a loud chirping, rasping noise, which is deafening close at hand and clearly audible up to its range in km away. This noise can be used to transmit messages to all cult initiates within earshot. The message is not understandable to anyone else.

spells.name: Invigorate
spells.description: Invigorate restores the Fatigue Level of the target, up one level per 2 points of Intensity in the spell. This only has an effect upon Fatigue lost to tiredness or effort, not levels lost to blood loss or the like.

spells.name: Jabbers
spells.description: This spell turns one of the recipient's arms, or optionally tail, in which case the tail cannot use its normal attack such as inject poison, into a jabber, a long bony point used for fighting. It has a weapon Size of Medium, Reach of Long, can impale, Armour Points equal to the spell's Intensity and does 1d10 damage plus any Damage Modifier. The recipient can attack and parry with the jabber using their Unarmed skill.

spells.name: Keenclaw
spells.description: Both the recipient's hands become paws as per a lion (RuneQuest page 369), doing 1d8 damage plus his Damage Modifier and using his normal Unarmed skill to strike. The magically hard claws are not only capable of inflicting the Bleed and Flurry special effects, but the recipient can use Sunder to tear away hide or armour. The claws are clumsy and cannot hold weapons or tools.

spells.name: Knowledge
spells.description: This spell allows the user to read the past history of an item. If the information cannot be assimilated fully within the duration of the spell, parts may be lost. If the item has a long history, the spell starts at the beginning unless the user dictates a particular time at which to start.

spells.name: Lava Spear
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a spear. It permits the wielder to fire splatters of molten rock from the tip of the spear by expending magic points. For every Magic Point spent, a gout of lava flies out, inflicting 5d6 damage on impact. Armour protects, but shields which parry the lava are subject to its burning damage. The lava has a range of 100 metres, and uses the wielder's spear combat style to hit.

spells.name: Leap
spells.description: Leap allows the caster to travel up to 10 times the Intensity in kilometres, by leaping in an arc through the Otherworld to any destination he or she desires. Multiple castings can be combined together to allow additional followers touching the caster to be brought along. The landing place must be known in advance and the trip, no matter its distance, takes a full day.

spells.name: Leaping Legs
spells.description: When cast upon a leaping insect this increases its leaping ability by 10m per point of Intensity either horizontally or vertically. When cast upon a non-insect, it turns his hind legs into enormous, jointed, bug-like limbs, allowing the recipient to leap half the same distance.

spells.name: Lie
spells.description: This spell is undetectably cast at the same time that the caster tells a lie. Anyone hearing the lie who fails to resist automatically believes it to be true, no matter how outrageous, and continues to believe it until they have incontrovertible evidence of its falsehood. For instance, a trickster used this spell to tell his friends that the Sun wasn't going to rise tomorrow; it would elicit a great deal of panic until next morning or until a Yelm priest performed Divination and asked his deity what had happened.

spells.name: Lightning
spells.description: Use the default Lightning miracle (RuneQuest page 272).

spells.name: Lion's Head
spells.description: This spell begins at the bottom of the page and continues on the next page. This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a lion. He gains a Bite attack as per a lion (RuneQuest page 369), doing 1d10 damage plus his Damage Modifier. This bite attack can be used in addition to normal weapon attacks and parries, provided the opponent is within reach.

spells.name: Living Blade
spells.description: A recipient under the influence of this spell move with lightning speed in combat, gaining 1 additional Action Point per Round.

spells.name: Lock
spells.description: This spell has been renamed to Seal (page 109) to avoid confusion with the Folk Magic spell of the same name.

spells.name: Locust's Head
spells.description: The recipient's head transforms into that of a giant locust, gaining a number of points of natural armour equal to half the Intensity of the spell, replacing any previous natural armour if greater. This transmogrification usually displaces any helmet not designed for an insectile head of that size. In addition, the recipient gains the ability to chew up and digest almost anything, providing it is not immediately destructive, such as molten metal or glass.

spells.name: Madness
spells.description: As per the default Madness miracle (RuneQuest page 272), save that the result of the opposed roll has a more significant effect. If the target is overcome, refer to the Madness table to judge the results of their Willpower roll.

spells.name: Magical Arms
spells.description: Each use of the spell creates a magical limb, usually an arm, attached to the torso of the caster. The formed limb is fully capable as per a normal humanoid arm or leg in terms of supporting weight or manipulating objects. This spell can be used to temporarily replace a missing limb or add additional ones beyond the norm. However, the extra limbs do not grant any bonus Action Points; nor, being magical creations, do they count as physical Hit Locations and therefore have no Hit Points.

spells.name: Magnify Command
spells.description: Magnify Command must be stacked with a Command spell (page 83). It boosts the duration and range beyond those of the base spell, and forces the commanded creature to suffer a penalty of one Difficulty Grade when resisting the spell.

spells.name: Mating Ritual
spells.description: This spell is normally cast by a female creature of Chaos upon a single male of any humanoid species. The spell allows the two to mate, although the fertility of the union is not guaranteed. The ritual need only last a single hour to achieve its purpose, but the 'wife' of the captive usually makes full use of the entire duration. The ritual inevitably ends in the painful death of the 'husband', whether through wounds, terror or raw exhaustion. When the 'husband' is a human, the viability of the union is almost assured, resulting in pregnancy if the caster can roll equal or less than the man's Endurance. With other races the chances are poorer, a roll suffering one Difficulty Grade, dwarves two grades and three grades for an elf. A successful pregnancy results in a birth or laying of a single egg. The emerging offspring is always female and bears some resemblance to her father, but does not deviate far from the form of her mother.

spells.name: Matrix Creation
spells.description: All spell matrix enchantments are now created by performing cult specific Heroquests to the other world.

spells.name: Meld Form
spells.description: This day-long ritual must be undergone to make the spell Proteus effectual (see page 105). A living physical creature of the type desired by the supplicant must be provided, although its SIZ cannot exceed three times the Intensity of the spell. When the ritual ends, the creature vanishes completely, and the supplicant forever-after gains the ability to turn into that creature by use of the Proteus spell. If the sacrificed creature is sapient, it gains a chance to resist the magic using its Willpower. If this fails, the creature's soul is not destroyed, and goes to whatever afterlife it deserves when its body vanishes. Non-sapient creatures, those with INS, cannot resist.

spells.name: Mind Blast
spells.description: Use the default Mindblast miracle (RuneQuest page 272).

spells.name: Mind-Read
spells.description: If not resisted, this spell gives the caster the ability to read the conscious thoughts of a target without his consent, although he will know his mind is being read. It cannot be used to trawl through the victim's memories - only their current surface thoughts.

spells.name: Mindlink
spells.description: Use the default Mindlink miracle (RuneQuest page 272). Shared spells require both users to possess the required Rune Affinity.

spells.name: Minor Infection
spells.description: The caster can infect a target with one of the many minor diseases known to the cult. Those that fail to resist the magic must compare the result of their Endurance roll on the Infection Severity table to judge how serious their infection is, then roll on the Minor Infection table if a random disease is required. All the diseases are described in the boxed text on page 143.

spells.name: Mist Cloud
spells.description: This spell creates a small area of natural-looking mist around a target, which if hostile, may try to resist. Failure means that the cloud will follow the target. Visibility in or through the mist is limited to arm's length, effectively blinding the recipient if they possess no other senses to help.

spells.name: Mold Rock
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to use his or her hands like hammers, chisels and files, to shape rock. Mold Rock can also be used to dig tunnels or undermine foundations. Shaping Truestone or Adamant is impossible, unless the caster has some form of cult gift or Feat which permits him to do this.

spells.name: Morale
spells.description: Establishes a field around the caster which will affect the entire military regiment or members of the same cult with an unbreakable esprit de corps. Not only are the recipients immune to fear of any kind, automatically winning any Morale Test if using the battle rules from Ships and Shield Walls, but they may also augment their Combat Style by raising it by one Difficulty Grade. Thus a regiment of Humakti swordsmen unaffected by any situational penalties raise their combat skills from Standard to Easy.

spells.name: Moth's Head
spells.description: Whenever this spell is cast, the recipient's head becomes identical to that of a large furry moth, with long feathery antennae. Using these sensitive receptors he gains a Track skill equal to the value of the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. In addition the recipient may automatically pinpoint the exact direction, number and distance of living organisms when within a distance of 10 times the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Moulder
spells.description: This spell causes a mushroom, mildew, mould, slime, or other fungus infection to infest everything within the area. The mould affects inanimate material such as wood, brick, stone, plants and even cloth, by reducing its Hit Points by an amount equal to the Intensity of the spell, crumbling it away. Thus a rope could be eaten through entirely, or a wooden door weakened by rot. Living creatures caught within the area suffer no ill effects from being infested, although their clothes and equipment might.

spells.name: Multispell
spells.description: This magic allows multiple Folk Magic spells to be cast simultaneously with the Multispell, saving the caster vital actions during times of combat. One Folk Magic spell can be cast for each four points of the Multispell's Intensity. It does not reduce the Magic Point costs of the spells, merely allows them to all be cast at the same moment as the Multispell, costing 1 Action Point, whether or not they are progressive. The only restriction is that duplicate Folk Magic spells cannot be cast multiple times on the same target. For example an initiate who cast Multispell at Intensity 9 could on the same Turn decide to simultaneously cast a Bladesharp 2 on himself and fire off two Disruption 3 spells, the latter of which must strike separate targets. This would cost a punitive 8 Magic Points, but might well take his foes by surprise!

spells.name: Oath
spells.description: Binds two or more people in physical contact into a mutually sworn pact, backed up by the threat of divine retribution. The maximum number of participants is equal to the Intensity of the spell, of whom the caster may or may not include himself. Oath is always cast in concert with a second spell, whose effect is held in check by the cult deity. If thereafter anyone breaks the oath, they are immediately subject to the spell at double the caster's relevant Rune Affinity. For example, an Oath spell cast by an Initiate of Humakt would be combined with Sever Spirit, promising a swift death for oath breakers. If the caster had a Death Rune affinity of 75%, then whoever broke his word would be subject to a Sever Spirit spell cast at 150% (double the Rune Affinity for the embedded retributive spell). Although the duration of the oath depends on the terms sworn, the Devotional Pool cost of both the Oath and its additional spell are not considered enduring and can be recovered normally.

spells.name: Pain Blow
spells.description: This spell must be cast on a weapon. It causes any hit that penetrates armour to potentially incapacitate the location struck in addition to normal damage. The effect can be resisted by the victim, provided they win an opposed test of their Endurance against the attack roll of the wielder. If the damage resulted in a Serious Wound or worse, that Hit Location is automatically incapacitated.

spells.name: Pain Tooth
spells.description: Use Pain Blow above, but natural weapons can be affected also.

spells.name: Panic
spells.description: If the target fails to resist, they are seized with an overpowering terror and cannot cast any spells or attack in melee, though he can parry or evade normally. Usually, the victim runs away.

spells.name: Passage
spells.description: This spell must be stacked with Lock, or with Seal (page 109). Each point of Intensity allows one additional person to pass through or open the magically sealed aperture besides the caster. It is cast on the object or portal whilst the additional persons are present, laying their hands on the opening as the spell is intoned. These additional persons cannot boost the priest's Lock spell with their magic points unless in Mindlink with him.

spells.name: Path Watch
spells.description: Used when passing through dangerous or questionable territory, it must be laid upon a known path or a visible road which will be travelled by the caster. Whilst the caster remains on that path, the spell will alert him of the presence of all traps and enemies, though not their type, within the radius of effect.

spells.name: Pathway
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to determine the direction of the nearest oasis. For every two points of Intensity the next nearest oasis can be sensed. Thus if Pathway was cast at Intensity 6, directions to the nearest three oases are known. The spell tells only direction and order of closeness, not the actual distance.

spells.name: Peace
spells.description: This spell causes all persons not of Devotee status within the area to lay down their weapons and forget all violence and war. For the length of the spell they prefer rather to listen to the wonders of peace and love which the caster sends ringing through their minds.

spells.name: Plant Eye/Plant Spy
spells.description: A specially chosen plant may be used as a spy by converting the leaves to sound and touch transmission devices without altering their appearance. Additional plants can be prepared for use with this spell, up to a number equal to the Intensity of the spell. Whilst in use the caster may swap between the chosen plants, but only spy out of one at a time. If any of the specially prepared plants are cut or uprooted, they cease transmitting information.

spells.name: Plastron
spells.description: This spell gives the recipient's body a carapace or shell over his chest and abdomen. This natural armour has no effect on Strike Rank or the user's Swim skill, but it does prevent him from wearing any other armour over the torso. In return it grants a number of Armour Points equal to one and a half times the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Power Drain
spells.description: Power Drain grants the caster the ability to drain Magic Points in hand to hand combat. The caster's touch will reach through an opponent's armour forcing them to resist in an opposed test of Willpower. If the caster wins, they drain a rolled amount of Magic Points equal to the Intensity of the spell, as per the Earthpower Effect table (page 92). The caster receives none of the drained power however, with all of the Magic Points flowing through them directly to the cult deity instead.

spells.name: Precision (Specific Skill)
spells.description: There are many variants of this spell, each one only usable with a specific skill. Thus, there exists Precision Boating, Precision Evade, Precision Stealth and so forth. When the recipient succeeds in the appropriate skill roll, a 1d100 result that would normally be a simple success becomes a critical success. Criticals, failures and fumbles are unchanged.

spells.name: Predict Weather
spells.description: When this spell is cast, the user knows what the natural weather will be like for the following 3 x Intensity hours in the region covered by the magic. Predict Weather cannot account for the actions of gods, demons or the use of weather-affecting spells.

spells.name: Proteus
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to change his shape into that of one creature with which he has undergone the Meld Form ritual (page 102). The user's INT and POW do not change, but all other characteristics become identical to those of the creature used in the Meld Form ritual. The user can also use special innate powers of the creature he has become. For the duration of the spell, the user can transform back and forth between his usual shape and the chosen creature's. Each transformation takes one melee round, during which the caster can do nothing else. If the caster stacks more than one point, he can alternate between his available shapes. Bremildaker has used the Meld Form ritual to gain the ability to turn into a sardine, an octopus and a small sea serpent. If he casts three concurrent incidences of the Proteus spell, he can switch back and forth between his own shape, the sardine's, the octopus' and the sea serpent's. Thus, he could become a sardine, then an octopus, then himself, then the sea serpent, then the octopus again, if he wished, for the spell's duration.

spells.name: Purification Ritual
spells.description: This night-long ritual is used to cleanse female trolls who have birthed a litter of trollkin. Normally, any female troll who gives birth to trollkin will henceforth give birth to trollkin in all future pregnancies. A female undergoing this ritual and avoiding pregnancy for a full year following the ritual is cleansed and can once again give birth to normal troll children, though there is no guarantee that she will do so.

spells.name: Purify Water
spells.description: This spell causes all particulate matter and contaminants in a 10 m3 per point of Intensity volume of water to settle. It will work on foreign minerals dissolved in the water, such as salt, including poisons, making the water perfectly transparent and otherwise safe to drink. The spell is often used to make tainted oases or chaos infested swamp water drinkable for men and beasts alike.

spells.name: Radiate
spells.description: This allows the caster's body to radiate intense heat, capable of burning anything which is touching it with 1d6 damage per two points of Intensity in the spell. This radiant heat reduces by 1d6 per metre distance from the body and armour counts against it. Weapons striking the caster must first suffer the burning damage to see if they survive long enough to inflict their own harm. Thus a javelin (3AP/8HP) thrown at someone under the aegis of an Intensity 11 Radiate would have to survive 6d6 damage before it struck, likely causing it to burst into fire and turn to ash before touching the caster.

spells.name: Rain
spells.description: This spell can only be cast when the sky is overcast or at least 50% clouds. Torrential rain, 1mm per minute, will then fall within the area, reducing visibility, soaking clothes and bowstrings, and potentially turning the ground into a slippery morass. When the spell ends, the rainfall stops.

spells.name: Raise Ghoul
spells.description: Raise Ghoul may be used on any 'fresh' corpse which has lain dead no longer than a number of days equal to half the Intensity of the spell. It recalls the original spirit before it can be judged and sent to the afterlife, binding it back into its own rotted body. The transformed ghoul claws out of the grave or mausoleum the following night, adopting its original Characteristics when still alive, but adjusted as described in the RuneQuest Core Rules (RuneQuest page 355). Despite creating the ghoul, this spell grants the caster no control over the undead creature.

spells.name: Rebirth of Chaos
spells.description: When cast on someone who lacks any chaos features, it permits them to roll twice on the Chaotic Features table (page 209) and choose which one they will take. If, on the other hand, the spell is cast on somebody who already has one or more features, it causes the recipient to re-roll all their chaotic features on the table, permanently changing them, plus receive the bonus of gaining an additional feature of their choice. There is a danger in this. The recipient has a percentage chance of dying equal to 5% plus 5% more per chaotic feature he already has. For example, a broo already with two chaos features stands a 15% chance of death if the spell is cast upon him. Unwilling recipients are allowed to resist using Endurance.

spells.name: Reconstruction
spells.description: This spell causes a sequence from the past, equal in length to the spell's duration, to replay for the caster's senses, as long as he or she is in the immediate area in which it took place, or is in Mindlink with someone who is currently there. Whilst the spell lasts, the caster is in a trance state and no one else can observe what they sense. The user must state the time and date of starting the vision.

spells.name: Recover Spirit
spells.description: This spell must be used within one day of death, and requires the caster to meet the victim's spirit on the spirit plane and force it back to its body by succeeding in one round of Spirit Combat with the departing spirit. However, after one day the spirit will have departed on the journey to the halls of the dead to await judgement, and the spell will not work. The body must first be healed of any Major Wounds. A fumble results in the caster finding the wrong spirit to put back in the body. Use the Ancestral Summons table on page 99 to determine the replacement spirit found.

spells.name: Reduce Flame
spells.description: See Suppress Oakfed (page 117).

spells.name: Refine Medicine
spells.description: This Rune spell is usually utilised in concert with the Find Healing Plants skill, to create an enduring herbal cure for a particular disease, thus saving the recipient of the medicinal powder, paste or whatever from needing to know the Cure Disease spell; however, the Healing skill is still necessary to apply it to an ill victim. Despite the instantaneous nature of the spell, the refined medicine so created lasts for a number of weeks equal to the Intensity it is cast at.

spells.name: Reflection
spells.description: Use the default Reflection miracle (RuneQuest page 274).

spells.name: Regrow Limb
spells.description: This spell regenerates a severed or maimed limb or appendage, taking one week per missing Hit Point to grow back (see Permanent Injuries, RuneQuest page 122). The spell must be maintained until completion, otherwise the partially regenerated limb will shrivel away.

spells.name: Regrow Organ
spells.description: As per Regrow Limb, save that it affects an organ such as an eye or tongue instead. Due to the smaller size of the area affected, Regrow Organ only takes a week to complete.

spells.name: Release Intelligence
spells.description: This spell only affects creatures which possess INS. It is the opposite of the spell Fix Intelligence (page 94), directly converting its INS characteristic to INT. The personality and memories of the creature remain as before, but it gains the ability to reason, learn knowledge and communication skills and use magic. Creatures unwilling to be awakened to sapience may attempt to resist using Willpower. This spell completely cancels the effects of the Fix Intelligence spell.

spells.name: Remove (Specific Body Part)
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to pull off the specified limb or organ without damage or pain. The detached limb can still be moved and the caster receives normal sensory input from it. Someone with Remove Hand could take off their hand and send it crawling away. Even if the user cannot see his limb, he can still control it and feel what it feels, though he may not be able to tell where or how far away it is. If the separated limb is damaged, all normal rules ensue as if it were not detached, thus the owner can become incapacitated, or even be poisoned. Replacing the missing part with a spell such as Regrow Limb automatically fails, unless the old part is destroyed. The user can reaffix the detached part if he brings it back within the spell's duration. If the spell ends before it is restored, it must remain separated until he casts the spell again.

spells.name: Reproduce
spells.description: Use the default Fecundity miracle (RuneQuest page 270), save that the spell can also affect a plant, for example an apple tree.

spells.name: Resist Iron Burn
spells.description: When cast upon a member of the Aldryami or Uz race, this spell grants the recipient a brief respite from their vulnerability to Iron weapons. In effect it negates the increased damage of iron weapons against those Elder Races. Resist Iron Burn replaces the original spell of Cure Iron Burn for ease of play and added survivability.

spells.name: Restore Health
spells.description: This spell restores Characteristic points that have been lost to disease or to the effects of Tapping (see XX). It does not however cure any disease(s) that may be present. The number of Characteristic Points recovered is equal to the Intensity of the spell. Unlike previous versions of this spell, Restore Health is not restricted to a specific Characteristic, but it is recommended that availability is limited to cults possessing the Harmony Rune.

spells.name: Restore Vision
spells.description: This spell restores normal sight to the target, whether it was lost permanently or simply through the temporary use of a spell such as Blinding. If the eyes themselves were destroyed, a Regrow Organ must be cast on each eye prior to casting Restore Vision.

spells.name: Resurrect/Resurrection
spells.description: Use the default Resurrect miracle (RuneQuest page 274).

spells.name: Reverse Chaos
spells.description: A target who fails to resist must roll on the detrimental Reverse Chaos table, and suffer the listed effect. Once the spell concludes however, it does not taint its target permanently with Chaos.

spells.name: Ride River Horse
spells.description: When cast at the banks of a watercourse, this spell summons a River Horse, which can be mounted and ridden on a trip from one headwater to anywhere else on that river, journeying through the watery realms of the Otherworld. The caster must have a clear image of where he wishes to travel to, or it will deposit him at some random place. The River Horse may bear as many passengers as the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Ritual of Devouring
spells.description: This spell is ritually cast before killing and devouring a sapient victim. Properly done, this spell enables whoever devours the victim to gain knowledge. Each participant in the grisly feast must eat 1d3 SIZ points of the victim to gain benefits. The eater gains 1d6 percentiles in any skill in which the victim was at least 25 percentiles better than the eater. For each Folk Magic spell the victim knew, the eater may roll his Willpower or less on 1D100. Success allows the eater to gain knowledge of that spell. No higher magic can be obtained through this ritual, unless both the victim and eater share the same cult. The maximum number of participants in the feast cannot exceed the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Ritual of Rebirth
spells.description: During the spell's duration, any victim eaten by the caster will, a few days later, be laid in an egg. One season after the devouring, the eggs will hatch and the being or beings devoured under the spell will be reborn as a scorpion man, scorpion elf, scorpion baboon, etc., depending on the species originally eaten. The upper torso and head will remain identical to the one devoured, whilst the lower body will now be that of a scorpion. The victim recalls all previous skills, and uses his Unarmed skill to attack with his new tail.

spells.name: Safe
spells.description: This spell is cast on a container or opening to bar unwanted passage. When anyone other than the caster, or someone in Mindlink with the caster, tries to open the secured container or cross a doorway with this spell, he is magically attacked. If the victim fails to resist, he takes as much damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (worn armour does not protect) to a random Hit Location, and is forced back. If, on the other hand, the target is not overcome, the spell is broken and he may enter freely, as may anyone following. If he fails to overcome the spell on his first try, he can try again and again until he either succeeds or is slain.

spells.name: Sanctify
spells.description: Use the default Consecrate miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

spells.name: Sapience
spells.description: Sapience grants a bonus to all INT-based skills, raising their chance by one difficulty grade for the duration of the spell. In addition, if progressive Folk Magic is used, it raises its Intensity cap as if the caster's INT had been increased by the Magnitude of the Sapience.

spells.name: Seal
spells.description: A more powerful version of the Lock Folk Magic spell, which works on any object or item which can be 'closed'; i.e. a door, box, chest and even a book! Seal provides the shut object with a number of Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell. Any tool or weapon used to break it is subject to the rules for Using Weapons Against Inanimate Objects (RuneQuest page 122). Additionally, the caster may open and close the sealed object at will without needing to recast the magic.

spells.name: Seal Spirit
spells.description: Use Recover Spirit (page 106), save that the spirit of the deceased must be defeated in the first round of Spirit Combat.

spells.name: Seal Tongue
spells.description: This night-long ritual must be performed over a willing recipient and lasts many months. It helps during interrogations when resisting any truth-detecting or truth-enforcing spell of equal or less Magnitude. Any time the recipient becomes the focus of such magic, they may attempt to resist the magic using their Willpower, whether or not the truth spell normally grants a resistance roll. If the recipient successfully resists, the spell has no effect on him. If he fails however, he dies revealing nothing.

spells.name: Seal Wound
spells.description: This spell is cast upon a weapon. Damage inflicted by the weapon cannot be cured with magic or chaotically regenerate, but may only recover naturally, using the victim's Healing Rate (RuneQuest page 121). A Heal or stronger spells will still stop blood loss, but do not restore any damage. The skills of First Aid and Healing are not affected by this spell.

spells.name: Seastrength
spells.description: This spell doubles the recipient's base STR for the purpose of boosting their Damage Modifier. It has no effect on the value of STR based skills. Thus someone with STR 15 and SIZ 16 would recalculate their Damage Modifier with STR 30 and SIZ 16, increasing it from +1d4 to +1d10. The target must be in contact with seawater when the spell is cast.

spells.name: Second Mouth
spells.description: This spell may only be cast upon a woman. It renders her practically immune to rape, by permitting her to take immediate revenge upon the assailant; incidentally inflicting damage to the rapist's abdomen as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table. If this results in a Serious Wound or worse, the member is bitten clean off.

spells.name: Sever Spirit
spells.description: Use the Sever Spirit miracle (RuneQuest page 275).

spells.name: Shadows Dance
spells.description: This spell requires the recipients to dance in a great ceremony for at least an hour prior to receiving its effect. All participants in the dance receive a special benefit increasing the damage they inflict in Spirit Combat to that indicated by referencing each individual's Darkness Rune affinity on the Spirit Damage table (RuneQuest page 201).

spells.name: Shake Earth
spells.description: Use the Earthquake miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

spells.name: Shape Metal
spells.description: This spell allows the user to use his hands like hammers, files and drills to shape metal. It is equally usable with any and all Rune metals. If the metal is hot, the user will take no damage.

spells.name: Shattering
spells.description: This spell acts as a powerfully augmented Disruption spell. If the target fails to resist, it inflicts 1d3 damage plus an additional bonus equal to the Intensity of the spell. Thus a Shattering cast at Intensity 8 would inflict 1d3+8 damage to a single Hit Location.

spells.name: Shield
spells.description: Each point of this spell serves as either 1 point of physical or magical protection, as desired by the caster. Physical defence counts as Armour Points, whilst the magical defence blocks spells of an equivalent numerical Magnitude and lower. Thus a Shield spell cast at Intensity 7 could provide 7AP, or 7 points of magical defence, or a combination thereof. The physical protection provided does not stack atop worn armour, rather it supplants it in those areas less protected, although it will work against certain spells, those that normally bypass natural and worn armour, such as Lightning.

spells.name: Shield of Darkness
spells.description: As per Shield, but adds the darkening effect of the Folk Magic spell Darkwall (page 64).

spells.name: Shooting Star
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a single arrow. When fired it starts to burn, leaving a glowing trail behind, and splits into as many arrows as the Intensity of the spell, each one targeting a different victim. The caster makes a single attack roll using their relevant Combat Style, applying the result to all the victims simultaneously. The Hit Location however is rolled separately for each target. The arrows are treated as if under the effects of a Firearrow spell, but cannot provide a lingering penalty if used to impale, as they are consumed by the flames.

spells.name: Sidana's Blessing
spells.description: Similar to Reproduce, this spell when cast on a single living creature of any race, permits the caster to ensure it will bear or sire offspring through sexual congress. If the union is incestuous, the spell will succeed to the greatest degree possible, either producing twins or permitting the Characteristics of a sole offspring to be increased. In the latter case, one die per Characteristic may be exchanged for its maximum value. For example a human offspring would replace the 3d6 roll for STR with 2d6+6, and so on with the other Characteristics. To be successful, Sidana's Blessing must be maintained for the entire pregnancy. The spell does not protect a female broo from the usual consequences of broo birth.

spells.name: Silence/Silence Sphere
spells.description: This spell creates a barrier to sound around the perimeter of its area, which is centred upon the target. No sound can pass this barrier. Characters inside can hear each other, but can hear nothing from outside, and vice versa. If the spell is cast at an unwilling or unknowing enemy, they may resist.

spells.name: Silver Track
spells.description: This spell calls upon the stars to illuminate a path or set of tracks the caster wishes to follow. Providing at least one star is visible in the sky, the path shows up in the caster's vision with a faint silvery glitter, doubling her Track skill and permitting her to move at full speed if the night sky is clear, or half speed if somewhat overcast.

spells.name: Skin Broo
spells.description: This two-stage magic spell begins with a ritual that may only be cast on a cult holy night and lasts up to a number of hours equal to the spell's Intensity. A broo captive is brought before the caster, who must torture, slay and skin it, then use its skin to make a drum. This magical instrument can only be activated once per night, anytime after sunset. Whilst the drum is playing, any broo within spell range who can hear it will be Demoralized as the Folk Magic spell, towards whatever the caster decided when fashioning the drum: darkness, trolls, etc. The broo can resist by winning an opposed test of their Willpower against the Musicianship skill of the playing caster. The spell must be maintained by the caster to preserve the drum's enchantment, but once the duration expires, the skin rots away to a putrid mess and the captive soul of the sacrificed broo will be released as a disease spirit.

spells.name: Sky Crater Fury
spells.description: This spell increases the caster's chance to attack with weapons by one Difficulty Grade. Thus a Lunar warrior unfettered by any other Situational Modifiers would normally have an attack chance of Standard, but under this spell it would be raised to Easy. Conversely the caster suffers a penalty of one Difficulty Grade to any parry or evade he attempts. Additionally the user cannot cast magic other than attack spells: Disruption, Smother, Mindblast and so on.

spells.name: Slash
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon an axe. It provides a bonus to the axe damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109).

spells.name: Smoldering Rebellion
spells.description: This spell has not been converted due to its abstraction and difficulty of use in play.

spells.name: Snow
spells.description: This spell must be cast when precipitation is present. If it is raining, the spell changes all the rain within the spell's radius of effect to snow, sleet or hail as decided by the Gamemaster. If it is already snowing, the spell doubles the amount of snow falling.

spells.name: Solace
spells.description: When this spell is cast, it acts to cancel mental distress from everyone within the area of effect, whether from grief over the loss of a loved one, from fear of death or whatever. The effect is to relieve stress on the victim and enable him or her to go about normal actions. It does not relieve pain or incapacitation, such as from wounds, but it does enable them to face the prospect of death bravely.

spells.name: Soul Sight
spells.description: Use the Soul Sight miracle (RuneQuest page 275).

spells.name: Soulfire
spells.description: Soulfire augments any Disruption spells cast by the recipient, adding +1 to damage for every two points of Intensity. So under an Intensity 7 Soulfire, the damage of Disruption spell is increased to 1d3+4.

spells.name: Spawn Crawling Hand
spells.description: This ritual must be performed upon a deceased humanoid creature tied to the Man Rune. It detaches and animates one of the hands of the victim, irrevocably tainting it with Chaos. The hand gains a fanged mouth at the wrist and the ability to scuttle along on its fingers at a Movement Rate of 2. Until the spell expires, it can survive indefinitely if provided proper food, usually gorp and other spawn. The hand responds to spoken commands of its creator only, allowing it to be used as a messenger, spy or crawling assassin. The hand has STR of 1d3, SIZ of 1d2, 1 Hit Point and uses the Athletics, Brawn, Endurance, Evade and Unarmed skills of the deceased corpse. It is capable of leaping up to its STR in metres, and if it manages to grab the throat of someone it is sent to kill, it strangles with whatever Damage Modifier it had before the hand was detached.

spells.name: Speak to/with (Specific Creatures)
spells.description: This spell has many different cult specific variations such as Speak to Birds, Speak to Herd Beast, Speak with Insects, Speak to Scorpion, Speak with Wolves, etc. It allows the recipient to talk with one type of creature for the length of the spell. Deceit or Influence can be used if the creature needs convincing. The spell does not instill any intelligence to the creature, so it can speak only from its natural awareness.

spells.name: Special Lock
spells.description: This spell must be combined with Seal. When cast on a specific person, it allows the recipient to freely open the object or portal affected by the Seal spell.

spells.name: Spell Teaching
spells.description: This spell has been made superfluous by the normal process of learning Folk Magic from a cult or mentor (RuneQuest page 177). However, this still requires a week of ritual and training in a cult holy place during which time nothing else may be done except for eating and sleeping.

spells.name: Spell Trading
spells.description: This spell allows the caster the ability to trade one use of any Rune spell which they currently know, even spells that did not originate from their cult, in exchange for one use of any Rune spell known by an initiate or devotee of another cult. The trade must be voluntarily and mutually agreeable. Trading counts the same as casting the spell and the originator loses the requisite number of Magic Points from their Devotional Pool, which remain out of use until the person the spell was traded to eventually casts it. This spell cannot be used to trade another instance of Spell Trading. The Intensity and Magnitude of proffered Rune spells remain those of the original donator, however the values cannot exceed the Intensity of the Spell Trading used to exchange them. Spell Trading may only be used within the boundaries of a Create Market spell (page 87) and requires the symbolic passing of a token representing the magic. Thus a Shield spell would use a shield, a Humakt spell a knife, and a Summon Sylph a bag of air; whether someone will trade a particular Rune spell depends on the relationship of his cult to the trader, trust of that merchant and possible conflict of Runic associations.

spells.name: Spider Bite
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a gigantic arachnid. He gains Armour Points equal to half the Intensity of the spell on his head and a bite attack capable of inflicting 1d6 damage, plus any Damage Bonus. Bite attacks are Size M, Range S, and use the recipient's Unarmed skill to strike. If this was not bad enough, the bite injects venom with a Potency equal to the caster's Rune Affinity used to cast the spell, but otherwise has the same properties as Giant Spider venom (RuneQuest page 388).

spells.name: Spider Limbs
spells.description: This spell sprouts four extra limbs from the user's sides. One pair are extra legs, used to double the user's Movement Rate and granting the Adhering creature ability (RuneQuest page 312). The other pair are arms, with tiny claws, too small to hold any weapon but a net, which may be wielded at the user's normal skill if part of one of their Combat Styles, or may grapple instead. The user may simultaneously use other weapons in his 'real' arms. In effect, the recipient receives one free grapple or net attack per round, in addition to their normal combat actions. When a hit is scored against one of the recipient's limbs, a second dice roll should be made to see if it is their normal limb (high), or his additional spider one (low). The spider limbs have the same Hit Points as their contemporary one, but have natural chitin armour equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Spider's Head
spells.description: See Spider Bite (page 112).

spells.name: Spirit Block
spells.description: Use the Spirit Block miracle (RuneQuest page 276), with the proviso that this spell does not block the Spirits of Reprisal of the cult that provides it.

spells.name: Spirit Guardian
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a friendly and willing ancestral spirit. It sets up a mental connection, identical to Mindlink, between spirit and caster, allowing them to share magic and communicate freely. Until the spell ends, the spirit guardian will follow the caster about, protecting him from malicious spirit attacks in the Otherworld, or manifesting in the physical world when necessary. The largest Intensity of spirit which can be requested to act as a guardian is equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Spirit Melding
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a friendly spirit, requesting it to envelop the caster. Whilst melded, the spirit suffers any Magic Point loss the caster should have taken due to Spirit Combat. If this reduces the spirit to zero, it suffers the consequences of defeat, although the caster may release it from the spell before it reaches that stage. The largest Intensity of spirit which can be melded with is equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Sprout
spells.description: This causes a seed to immediately quicken and send forth roots. Over the course of the duration, the infant plant grows and expands as if one year had passed for every minute the spell lasts. This usually results in a mature sapling or bush with fully developed foliage. This spell affects any mostly intact seed, even those hundreds of years old, provided it has been protected against insects, fungus and extremes of temperature.

spells.name: Sprout Arms
spells.description: This spell causes two additional arms to sprout from the recipient's sides. This grants them 1 additional Action Point and permits the carrying of additional weapons or shields. The new arms have the same Hit Points as the user's normal ones, but lack any armour save what the recipient naturally possesses due to the thickness of hide, scales and so on. Any hit on an arm location should roll again to see if the blow lands on the original arm (high) or the additional one (low).

spells.name: Sprout Legs
spells.description: This spell causes an additional pair of legs to sprout from the sides of the recipient. The leg is usable as a normal appendage, and has the same Hit Points and natural armour as the other legs. Each additional pair adds 2m to the Movement Rate of the recipient, and provides additional stability if one or more of the original limbs have been rendered inoperable due to Serious Wounds.

spells.name: Sprout Tail
spells.description: This spell adds an additional tail alongside the original, if the recipient has one. Depending on its form, the tail usable as a normal appendage, and has the same Hit Points and natural armour as the other tail, or leg if no tail exists. The tail can be utilised in combat using the recipient's Unarmed skill, but unless it is a creature with a naturally dangerous tail, like a scorpionman, it will be limited to 1d3 damage and special effects such as Trip Opponent or Bash.

spells.name: Sprout Wings
spells.description: This spell grows a pair of very fast beating membranous wings from the recipient's back, allowing him to fly at a Movement Rate of 12m. Treat the wings as having the same Hit Points as the recipient's legs, but no Armour Points. Becoming over encumbered when flying will accumulate fatigue and slow down this speed as normal (RuneQuest page 116).

spells.name: Star Ward
spells.description: This spell resembles Warding, but instead of wands or totems, it uses pieces of smooth silver. It does 1d3 damage per two points of Intensity to incoming enemies who cross the perimeter. In addition, it acts as a Shimmer spell against incoming missiles. Unlike Warding, Star Ward provides no magic protection. The area inside the ward is bathed in a soft silver glow, so the area affected is always illuminated as if by a Light spell.

spells.name: Stinger
spells.description: This spell transforms the abdomen or hind-quarters of the recipient into that of a giant bee, complete with sting and natural armour to half the Intensity of the spell. The stinger can be used in combat using the Unarmed skill, has Size M, Reach M, does 1d8 damage and injects venom with a Potency equal to the caster's Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. This causes the Agony condition in the Hit Location stung.

spells.name: Stone-Biting
spells.description: This spell strengthens the teeth of the recipient, permitting them to chew through inanimate objects without breaking their dentition (RuneQuest page 123). It allows any bite attack to ignore a number of Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell and has several practical uses. Firstly it allows the user to chew through bonds, or even slowly excavate stone tunnels. It can also be used in combat against heavily armoured foes, providing the user has suitable bite damage.

spells.name: Stones to Kill
spells.description: This night-long ritual enchants a number of black rocks equal to the Intensity of the spell. When thrown at a Chaotic target that is hit, they take effect. The stones need not penetrate armour, and the target cannot resist the effect. Each stone is one-use and will cancel out a single chaos feature of the struck target for 1d6 hours. If the target has more than one chaos feature, the one affected is determined randomly. As part of the ritual, the caster must eat the heart of a dead troll.

spells.name: Strength of Basmol
spells.description: This spell swells the recipient's sinews and muscles, granting a leonine physique. His current Damage Modifier is increased by one step for each two points of Intensity.

spells.name: Strike
spells.description: This spell is cast upon a weapon. The next blow by that weapon, provided it is not parried, ignores all armour, both natural and worn.

spells.name: Strongblade
spells.description: This spell increases the Armour Points of a sword or dagger blade to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell. In addition it suffers no damage when striking inanimate objects (RuneQuest page 123).

spells.name: Strongnet
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a net, making it nigh unbreakable by normal means, including fire, effectively increasing its Armour Points to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell. Someone trapped in the net must escape by unwrapping itself, unless they are immensely strong enough to tear it.

spells.name: Submerge
spells.description: Each point of Intensity drags 10 SIZ of a floating creature, or its totalled corresponding mass on the Creature Size Equivalency table (RuneQuest page 327) if an inanimate object, under the surface of the water. If swamped, naturally non-buoyant objects will sink to the bottom. Unwilling creatures targeted by the spell may attempt a Swim roll to resist.

spells.name: Summon (Specific Species)
spells.description: Summons a particular species of creature deemed sacred to the cult to the presence of the caster. The total SIZ of creatures so affected must be equal or less than 20 times the Intensity of the spell. Thus an initiate of Basmol who casts Summon Lion at Intensity 7 would draw 140 SIZ worth of lions; about six of the beasts if average sized. The creatures must travel to the caster under their own steam, resulting in their arrival part way through the spell's duration. Neither are they under the caster's control once they do arrive, which may result in unpleasantness, depending on their nature. Creatures under control of others may attempt to resist the summoning, but in this case it is the rider, herder and so on, who must use an appropriate skill in the opposed test. A rider of a summoned beast would use their Ride skill against the casting roll for instance.

spells.name: Summon Ancestor
spells.description: This spell summons a randomly determined spirit from the Land of the Dead, as rolled on the Ancestral Summons table (page 99). Providing it is friendly, the caster can request a brief service from it as long as can be attempted before the summoning concludes. This usually entails entering Spirit Combat with a foe, or casting its magic in support. Neutral ancestors may need to be convinced by overcoming it in an opposed test of the caster's Customs or Influence against the spirit's Willpower. Hostile ancestors generally make a nuisance of themselves and provide sarcastic commentary about the ineptitude of the caster.

spells.name: Summon Dead
spells.description: This ritual must be performed over the corpse, grave or other remains of the individual being summoned. The name of the individual must also be known. If the person being contacted presently resides within Ty Kora Tek's vast Caverns of the Dead, his ghost rises from the earth at the end of the summoning, patiently waiting to speak with the summoner or be given a task to perform. In the latter case the ghost is treated as a haunt (RuneQuest page 406).

spells.name: Summon Elemental
spells.description: The spells of Summon Small, Medium and Large Elemental have been supplanted. Use the Elemental Summoning miracle (RuneQuest page 269).

spells.name: Summon Founder
spells.description: The Founders are now treated as greater spirits and can only be summoned via Spirit Magic. See Cult of Waha (page XX).

spells.name: Summon Guardian
spells.description: As per Summon Ancestor, but brings back the spirit of a dead member of that cult. The spirit so summoned possesses the spirit ability of Animate. Hostile spirits actively try to kill the caster, whether by Spirit Combat or using the dead body it is bound to.

spells.name: Summon Lodril
spells.description: This ritual spell must be cast at the lip of an active volcanic fissure or caldera. It takes an entire day to cast. Each point of Intensity in the spell gives a cumulative 1% chance of starting an eruption. Several casters can add their spells together to increase the total chance of success. Onset is delayed for several hours, allowing participants to escape the area, but once started the eruption lasts a number of days equal to the highest Intensity used in the ceremony, and the immediate effects extend for a like number of kilometres in a specific direction as decided by the leader of the ritual. Anything in this path is affected by a pyroclastic flow, causing damage as described for the Earthquake miracle of similar Intensity (RuneQuest page 269). After this, ash will fall over a region double that affected by the pyroclastic flow, precipitating at a rate of half a metre depth per day until the spell ends. This has the effect of reducing visibility to mere metres and potentially burying everything unable to leave the region.

spells.name: Summon Minion of the Snake Eater
spells.description: This summons a snake eater with a spirit Intensity equal to half that of the spell. It has an INT of 2d6+6, CHA of 3d6, the spirit ability of Spellcasting and the unique ability of Venom as per the Disease ability, but treated as a poison instead. These spirits always know their full INT worth of Folk Magic. Snake eaters have the heads of women and beautifully scaled shapeless sacs for a body.

spells.name: Summon River Horse
spells.description: See Ride River Horse (page XX).

spells.name: Sunspear
spells.description: Use the Sunspear miracle (RuneQuest page 276).

spells.name: Support
spells.description: Support will hold up a section of earthen or stone wall with a volume of 64 cubic Metres or less per point of Intensity, whether it is part of a fortification, building or subterranean cave system. This allows work to be done on the wall, protects against earthquakes or temporarily prevents it from subsiding due to the actions of sappers. This spell can be cast in an emergency upon a wall that has already started to topple or collapse. However in this circumstance, the duration is reduced to Rounds, permitting those in the immediate area to flee before being engulfed in the resulting rubble.

spells.name: Suppress Aether
spells.description: This spell creates a sphere centred on the target. Inside the area is darkness equivalent to a Summon Shadow spell. Anyone with a Fire Rune affinity who tries to cross the borders of the spell and fails to resist will automatically take damage to a single Hit Location, as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109), ignoring all armour. The sphere's boundary also acts as magical protection against Fire Rune spells crossing it, as a Shield spell (page 110) of equivalent Intensity.

spells.name: Suppress Lodril
spells.description: See Suppress Aether.

spells.name: Suppress Oakfed
spells.description: This spell is cast on flames of various sorts. If cast on a normal fire, it decreases the fire's Intensity by 1 for every two points of Intensity in the spell; if cast on Fire Rune magic such as Fireblade or Firespear, it immediately dismisses any spell of equal or less Intensity. It can even be utilised upon salamanders, reducing them in size by one cubic metre for each point of spell Intensity. However in this case, the salamander can resist with its own Willpower.

spells.name: Sureshot
spells.description: Use the Sureshot miracle (RuneQuest page 276).

spells.name: Swallow
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to swallow whole any object of a SIZ up to half the Intensity of the spell. Thus, an Intensity 7 spell would let the user swallow a small dog in one gulp. Anything may be consumed, from a boulder to a cactus, yet the caster remains unharmed even if he swallows actively dangerous substances such as fire, acid or poison. Any living being so consumed will die, but the caster must first grapple the victim successfully before they may be swallowed.

spells.name: Sweat Acid
spells.description: The recipient's skin secretes an acid which inflicts damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109). The recipient is not affected by his own acid, but anything he touches or wears treats damage against armour as if using the Sunder special effect. If struck by a weapon, he takes normal damage, but the weapon then suffers acid damage. Unwilling recipients may attempt to resist.

spells.name: Switch Places
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to trade places with a person, or an illusory apparition of a person, within line of sight. Living targets who do not wish to be affected by this spell may attempt to resist.

spells.name: Tambour
spells.description: This ritual spell is cast upon a drum, and requires the caster to play it ceaselessly for the entire duration. All those within the area become invested with a limitless endurance, provided they begin to dance and don't cease moving to the beat of the drum. In practical terms this means that the dancers never lose any Fatigue due to physical effort, permitting them to fight, run or work without tiring, provided they cap any skill being used by the value of their Dance skill.

spells.name: Tanglethicket
spells.description: Use the Entangle miracle (RuneQuest page 269).

spells.name: Telekinesis
spells.description: See Flight (page 95).

spells.name: Teleportation
spells.description: The caster may teleport to any spot within range that he can see, either on his own, through the eyes of a familiar or through a Mind Link spell. Teleportation carries the caster and his personal gear, up to an ENC equal to twice the Intensity, but multiple castings of the spell can be stacked to bring along others in physical contact with him. An unwilling victim can resist with Willpower.

spells.name: Thunderbolt
spells.description: This spell has been conflated into the now more effective Lightning (page 100).

spells.name: Tongue
spells.description: This spell allows the recipient's tongue to be used as a weapon. The tongue has a Size of S, Reach of VL, and has an attack chance equal to the recipient's Unarmed skill. If it strikes it inflicts 1d3 damage, plus an additional bonus equal to the Intensity of the spell.

spells.name: Transform Self
spells.description: This spell must be stacked with all three of a cult's Transform spells, for example for a follower of Telmor it must be cast with Wolfhide, Wolfrunning, and Wolf's Head. When these spells are combined with Transform Self, the user bodily transforms into a semi-divine beast or insect relevant to that cult. All of the spells have full effect and, additionally, last for hours instead of minutes. All of the normal abilities of the creature become usable by the caster for the duration of the spell, thus in the case of Gorakiki-beetle, the caster might gain the abilities of a Karrg Beetle: Dark Sense, Formidable Natural Weapons, Grappler and Savage.

spells.name: Translate
spells.description: This spell translates all words touched by the user's index finger whether cursive script, carved glyphs or knotted strings at a fast reading speed, about 500 written words per minute, while the spell operates, and leaves a lasting memory of the meaning afterwards. Untranslatable words are not translated, though the user receives an idea of the word's meaning.

spells.name: Tree Chopping Song
spells.description: This ritual, which is performed as a group song, influences every lay member or higher in the caster's cult that chants along with the spell caster. All those affected may use any type of axe against Aldryami at the same percentage chance as whichever of their Combat Styles includes mace or maul. No other weapon types are included. The caster must beat on dead logs with his or her maul or maces as part of the ritual. If not already expired, the spell ends at daybreak.

spells.name: Truespeak
spells.description: This spell compels the recipient to speak nothing but the truth for the duration and answer all questions in a literal manner. The caster must overcome the recipient in a new opposed roll to compel the truth-telling for each question. The target can refuse to speak at all, but if he does speak, and fails to resist, he must tell the truth. If combined with Mind-Read, this spell makes the caster a potent inquisitor.

spells.name: Truetune
spells.description: This spell must be cast upon a stringed instrument, which glows with a golden radiance under its effect. Whilst played, the caster boosts the Level of Success of any Musicianship skill roll by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical - whilst rolled Critical Success cannot be boosted higher. Under its influence, the caster enters a trance in which only the music exists, making them oblivious to all else.

spells.name: Turn Blow
spells.description: Whilst under this spell, any blow from a melee or ranged weapon that strikes the caster gives her attacker an equal amount of hit point damage to a randomly rolled Hit Location. Even the damage from weapon augmenting spells like Bladesharp is reflected. Although armour reduces damage of the original blow upon the caster, it does not help against the damage reflected back against the attacker. Example: Ramba the priestess casts this spell in a fight against an elf. The elf impales with his Bladesharp enchanted spear, doing 11 total points of damage: 7 from the spear, 3 from the Bladesharp, and 1 from his Damage Modifier, to Ramba's abdomen, where she has 5 points of armour. Ramba takes 6 points of damage to her abdomen. In return the elf takes the full 11 points of damage to a random location, which turns out to be his head. His 6 Armour Point helmet plus Protection spell do not count, and he dies from the resulting Major Wound.

spells.name: Turn Undead
spells.description: This spell affects one undead creature, skeleton, zombie, mummy, ghoul or vampire, for each point of Intensity. Any targeted undead creature which fails to resist must match their roll on the Turn Undead table to determine the effect.

spells.name: Turtle's Head
spells.description: This spell gives the recipient the head of a great turtle. The main advantage is that he is granted the creature ability of Hold Breath, and his head gains a number of natural Armour Points equal to half the spell's Intensity. Under the effect of this spell the user loses the power of speech.

spells.name: Unisonance
spells.description: This spell requires a pair of stringed instruments, usually harps, specially enchanted as part of a cult-specific Heroquest. When Unisonance is cast upon one of the instruments, it will resonate with the other with which it is paired. The caster and whoever plays the other instrument can then communicate back and forth musically.

spells.name: Venom Bite
spells.description: This spell adds venom to the recipient's bite attack, which takes effect should they either inflict damage on the victim or use the Inject Venom special effect (RuneQuest page 317). The effects of venom are cult specific, and should be created by the Games Master. The Potency of the venom is equal to the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. The Intensity of the spell limits which conditions it inflicts. Use the Poison Condition Cost table to reflect the spell's effect. Multiple conditions may be purchased if enough Intensity is available. Onset time is based upon the victim's resistance roll: on a Critical the disease takes an hour before manifesting, on a Success a minute, on a Failure the start of the next Round and on a Fumble it starts on the victim's next Turn. The venom's effect continues until cured or the victim eventually passes away. In cases such as Sapping or Maiming the incremental loss occurs at the same rate as onset time.

spells.name: Venom Boosting
spells.description: This simple but deadly spell supplants the Potency of the recipient's own venom with the value of the caster's Devotion skill, if it is higher than that of the venom. For example, Kerarch a devotee of Bagog has a Devotion skill of 108%. When she casts Venom Boosting upon one of her tribe, it boosts the natural Potency of the scorpionman's venom to 108%, making it exceptionally difficult to overcome in an opposed roll.

spells.name: Vesper
spells.description: This spell causes the area to glow with a dull bloody red colour. Friends and allies of the caster within the glow may substitute the caster's Willpower for their own whilst engaged in Spirit Combat. In addition, the glow prevents characters from falling unconscious if all their Magic Points are expended, although it does not prevent any other effects of being defeated by a spirit.

spells.name: Vision
spells.description: Vision gives the caster a projected viewpoint, through using their own primary sense, usually sight. The viewpoint can travel anywhere within range, at a Movement Rate equal to the Intensity of the spell. However, the material through which the projected viewpoint can travel depends on the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. Thus Vision cast via the Water Rune may only move and see from bodies of water such as streams or pools. Whilst using Vision the caster cannot use his own senses, but may switch back and forth between the spell's viewpoint and his own from one round to the next.

spells.name: Vomit Acid
spells.description: The recipient user immediately vomits forth a quantity of steaming acid, which inflicts damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109). The caster is not affected by his own acid, but anything it touches is. The amount of magical acid produced is enough to cover all of a human sized target, affecting every Hit Location, fewer if the target is larger than human size, as decided by the Games Master. The target may resist using Evade to throw themselves clear.
