Shields may block damage to a number of locations as indicated in their traits, either page 57 or RuneQuest page 95, and will itself suffer the acid damage.

spells.name: Warding
spells.description: This ritual spell creates a place of safety for those inside. It requires at least three staves, totems or similar cult specific objects to mark out its perimeter - which need not be circular, provided each marker maps out the corners of the protected area lay within the maximum possible region. The volume extends up to half the spell's Intensity in metres above the ground. Once cast, the Warding is undetectable save for magical means. Any enemy, physical or spiritual, which attempts to enter this region from above, below or through the walls, triggers a loud noise, a keening, whistling, booming, etc., which may only be suppressed by the caster. In addition, unless the foe resists, it suffers 1d3 damage per two points of Intensity to a single Hit Location, or its total Hit Points if a spirit, ignoring armour. Lastly the Warding will block all spells of equal or lower Magnitude which cross its border.

spells.name: Warrior of Stone
spells.description: Causes a ritually prepared statue of stone and metal to awaken and serve the commands of the caster, so that it becomes capable of motion and combat. Use the Awaken miracle (RuneQuest page 264), treating its Armour Points as 10.

spells.name: Waste Loins
spells.description: If this spell is not successfully resisted, the target becomes forever sterile or barren - he or she can never again have any children.

spells.name: Water Skating
spells.description: See Waterwalk.

spells.name: Waterwalk
spells.description: This spell allows the recipient to move upon the surface of the water as if it were solid ground.

spells.name: Webbing
spells.description: This spell changes the recipient's abdomen into that of an arachnid. The posterior of this transformed location is a spinneret which, once per round, can hurl a mass of silver webbing towards a foe. This attack utilises the recipient's Unarmed skill in an opposed roll against the target's Evade skill. If the foe fails to throw himself clear, he is enveloped and held fast until he can escape or is cut free. Bursting the strands requires winning an opposed roll of the captive's Brawn against the caster's Devotion skill. The webs remain for several days after the spell concludes, permitting their use as a method of imprisonment, or as a cunningly laid trap if sprayed liberally across a floor.

spells.name: Whirlpool
spells.description: This spell creates a fearsome whirlpool spanning the area of effect. Anything caught in the whirlpool must successfully resist the spell using their Swim skill, or Brawn if still able to maintain footing or holding onto a branch or rope, else be sucked under. A victim caught in the whirlpool will be tumbled about until the spell ends, imprisoning them in the current and potentially drowning them as a result. If a boat or ship whose length is less than the diameter of the spell enters the whirlpool, its captain must resist the magic with a Boating or Seamanship test instead. If he does not win the opposed roll, the vessel is caught in the current and is whirled about. Worse still, if the Boating or Seamanship roll was a failure, the vessel suffers 1d6 damage to its Hit Points each minute. On a fumble it simply founders, sucked under the surface with the entire crew.

spells.name: Wind Walking
spells.description: This spell has been replaced by Spirit Magic. See Wild Hunter (page 146).

spells.name: Wind Warp
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to change the direction the wind blows, diverting it by up to 10 degrees per point of Intensity of the spell. Thus an Intensity 9 Wind Warp could divert the wind by 90 degrees. Small whirlwinds often form where the wind is forced to clash with itself. Assuming the ambient wind strength is a moderate breeze or stronger, these vortices can be used as a defence against ranged weapons, providing its area is reduced to the point where the boundary lies between the caster and any attackers. In this case Wind Warp will make the difficulty grade of ranged attacks one step harder.

spells.name: Wind Words
spells.description: Assuming there is a calm, this spell allows the caster to send his words on a zephyr to the ears of his target. If the ambient wind is a moderate breeze or stronger, his conversation can only be carried downwind. However, no matter how severe the winds are, the recipient still hears the caster as if standing next to him.

spells.name: Wither
spells.description: If this spell is not resisted it affects a randomly rolled Hit Location, which becomes permanently withered, deformed and useless. The location loses all Hit Points. If this is a vital location, such as the head, chest or abdomen, the victim dies. A withered limb cannot be healed, but it can be amputated and then Regrow Limb used to restore it properly.

spells.name: Wolf Hide
spells.description: This spell causes the recipient to grow a tail, to sprout a coat of grey fur and to gain partial immunity to damage, like a werewolf. Only magic, fire or iron will harm him. If an ordinary bronze sword with a Bladesharp spell is used against him, for example, only the Bladesharp would do damage. If the whole blade were enchanted, such as with a Truesword, the entire damage rolled, except for any damage bonus, would count. Poison still affects him if his skin is penetrated. The recipient still needs oxygen and thus can be asphyxiated or drowned.

spells.name: Wolf Running
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's limbs into those of a wolf, and permits him to run on all fours. Attempts to Sneak and Evade are one difficulty grade easier, with the additional advantage as described under Evading Creatures (RuneQuest page 152). His running speed increases to 8. He loses his hands, and so cannot perform any manual manipulation.

spells.name: Wolf's Head
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a large wolf. He gains a Bite Attack of Size M and Reach T, which inflicts 1d6 damage. This Bite Attack can be used in combination to normal attacks and parries, assuming that the foe is within range. The recipient uses his Unarmed skill to attack. Attempts to Track are one difficulty grade easier, unless the recipient lacks the skill in which case they temporarily gain the skill at base Characteristic value.

spells.name: Worm's Head
spells.description: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a worm. The exact effects of the spell depend upon whether the caster is standing on soil or in water when the spell is cast. If he is standing on solid rock or an artificial surface, the spell does not take effect.

spells.name: Worship
spells.description: This spell is no longer necessary to give worship to a divine being. Such things are performed as part of mundane ritual and ceremony which are performed daily by the priests of a cult.
